Summary: When do I get a 'You must call TIC before calling TOC' message?
Description: Before MATLAB R14sp2, there were two ways to generate this message. Starting with R14sp2, only Case 1 is possible and expected. Case 1. Using the toc  command before ANY usage of the tic  command. >> toc??? Error using ==> tocYou must call TIC before calling TOC. Case 2. Clearing the workspace between executing a tic  and toc  command. >> tic...>> clear all...>> toc??? Error using ==> tocYou must call TIC before calling TOC. To mark a starting time, the tic  command places the value for the current time in a global variable called TICTOC. The toc  command then uses the value of the TICTOC variable to compute the elapsed time. If, however, you were to clear this variable prematurely, as shown in the example for Case 2 above, toc  would not have the information it needs, thus causing MATLAB to throw an error.
Workaround: For Case 2, prior to R14sp2, do not use a clear all command between tic and toc commands.

Summary: Incorrect case in class name leads to segmentation violation
Description: If you provide a class-name argument with incorrect case to a function that operates on classes, a later call to such a function using the correct class name can result in a segmentation violation. For example: % Wrong case for class name COM.mscal_calendarmethods com.mscal_calendarNo methods for class com.mscal_calendar or no class com.mscal_calendar.% Correct case now results in a segmentation violationmethods COM.mscal_calendar...Stack Trace:   [0] mvalue.dll:public: void __thiscall Mimplementation::init(struct mxArray_tag *)(0, 0x012d5d20, 0x07ee29f0, 0x07dc9ea0) + 8 bytes   [1] m_dispatcher.dll:public: __thiscall Mlm_opaque::Mlm_opaque(class Mdispatcher *,char const *,bool)(0x077dc050, 0x07cc86b1 "com.mscal_calendar", 1, 0x07ee29f0) + 238 bytes ... This problem occurs on Windows platforms.
Workaround: Always use correct case for class names. For example, if the class name is COM.mscal_calendar, do not provide a class-name argument com.mscal_calendar.

Summary: SEGV in m_ir:mps_sweep_term_pools
Description: A seg-fault occurs in m_ir:mps_sweep_term_pools on 32-bit machines that allocate memory in the upper 2GB.
Workaround: The only workaround, if you are using MATLAB version 7.0.1 (R14SP1) or earlier, is to use less than 2G of memory. To avoid this problem altogether, upgrade to MATLAB version 7.0.4 (R14SP2).

Summary: Why does my color Logitech camera display black and white images?
Description: When I use a color camera from Logitech with the Image Acquisition Toolbox, the images displayed by the preview window or returned by getdata  are grayscale (black and white) instead of color. If I use the software supplied with the camera by Logitech, the images appear in color as expected.
Workaround: This problem occurs because some of the properties of the device are initialized to values at the extreme end of their range.  These properties are normally the Brightness, Contrast, and Saturation properties.  To workaround this problem these properties must be set to different values.To set the values of these properties, you must first get a handle to the video source object, which is a child of the video input object. To do this, use the following commands:% Create the videoinput object.vid = videoinput('winvideo', 1);% Get the videosource object.src = getselectedsource(vid);After getting a handle to the video source object, you can change the values of the properties using the graphical property editor or by using the set command. To use the graphical property editor, use the inspect command:inspect(src)The following code uses the set command to set the property values. To determine the allowable range of values for the property, use the propinfo command.  For example, to modify the Saturation property, use the following commands:% Determine the range of the property.info = propinfo(src, 'Saturation');% Set the property value to the middle of the range.set(src, 'Saturation', mean(info.ContraintValue));

Summary: SEGV error when loading MAT-file that contains user-defined data objects
Description: A bug with the handling of the RTWInfo.CustomAttributes  property in user-defined data classes with custom storage classes can cause a SEGV error when data objects of the user-defined class are loaded from a MAT-file. For user-defined data classes with custom storage classes, the RTWInfo.CustomAttributes  property is modified whenever the RTWInfo.CustomStorageClass  property is changed. However, if the CustomStorageClass  property is changed to specify a custom storage class that does not have any custom attributes, the CustomAttributes  property is not cleared, as it should be. This results in a SEGV error when a data object's RTWInfo  property is accessed.
Workaround: The following installation instructions may be used to apply the patch provided in the Attachments section to your MATLAB installation to prevent this issue from occurring.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB>toolbox/simulink/simulink folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB>toolbox/simulink/simulink folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>toolbox/simulink/simulinkunzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB>toolbox/simulink/simulink folder.Refresh the toolbox path cache, for example, by issuing the command rehash toolboxcache at the MATLAB prompt.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Inefficiency slows building of simulation target
Description: An inefficiency in the build process slows down the building of Accelerator simulation targets.

Summary: Accelerated Simulation produces incorrect results with Signal Storage Reuse on
Description: The Simulink Accelerator can produce wrong answers due to incorrectly generated code in which a local variable is overwritten before the variable's final use.
Workaround: Turning off the "Signal Storage Reuse" optimization avoids this bug.

Summary: Nonvirtual input ports: can cause segmentation violation during code generation
Description: If the following conditions exist, a segmentation violation can occur when Real-Time Workshop analyzes the model for data packing information during code generation: The model includes a nonvirtual subsystem. That subsystem has a Inport block that is connected directly to an Outport block. The model uses Simulink data objects in code generation.
Workaround: For a system that has a direct passthrough signal between an Inport block and Output block, insert a Gain block with a gain value of 1 between the the two blocks.

Summary: Linux KDE: MATLAB terminates or produces error messages after using Editor/Debugger or cut and paste
Description: On some Linux systems with KDE window managers, MATLAB terminates or produces error messages when you cut and paste into the Command Window or use the Editor/Debugger.
Workaround: Disabling Klipper (the KDE clipboard tool) prevents this bug. To disable Klipper permanently, do the following:Click the Klipper icon in your system tray on the right side of the task bar (it looks like a clipboard with a gray "K" on it)Select Quit.When prompted "Should Klipper start automatically when you login?", click No.To reenable Klipper (e.g., for use with other applications), click the K menu (leftmost icon on your task bar) and select System > Desktop Applet > Klipper.

Summary: Rician/Rayleigh channel blocks: incorrect ramp-up statistics
Description: Two blocks are affected by this bug: (1) the Rician Fading Channel block and (2) the Multipath Rayleigh Fading Channel block obsoleted in R14sp2.  The new Multipath Rayleigh Fading Channel block introduced in R14sp2 is not  affected.The two affected blocks are implemented using a filtered white noise source.  This filtering involves (a) a Doppler filter and (b) a cascade of interpolating filters.  A bug in the initialization of these filters causes incorrect fading statistics during the initial phase of a simulation.  The ensemble average of the square-magnitude of the fading envelope takes some number of samples to "ramp up" to the correct value.  The maximum error is about 10% of the correct power gain.
Workaround: As a workaround for the bug in the Rician fading block, you can instead use the new Multipath Rayleigh Fading Channel block (introduced in R14sp2; see "Fix") in parallel with a gain block representing the direct path, and sum their outputs.  The gain of the direct path should be set to a value sqrt(K) times that of the overall gain of the Rayleigh fading channel, where K is the Rician K-factor.  You can include an additional gain at the output of the sum block to normalize the overall power gain.  For example, if the Rayleigh fading block is set to normalize the overall gain, use a gain of sqrt(K) for the direct path and a gain of 1/sqrt(K+1) at the output of the sum block.  Moreover, you can use a frequency offset block in the direct path to simulate a Doppler shift on the line-of-sight component (direct path).  Finally, note that this workaround will work reliably only for a single-path fading model because of potential implementation delays introduced by the Rayleigh fading block.See attached model showing an example of this workaround.

Summary: Constant block: may cause TLC error when used in triggered subsystem
Description: If your model includes a Constant block in a triggered subsystem and you disable the Inline parameters  option on the Optimization  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, Real-Time Workshop may generate the following TLC error: Error: File: D:\MATLAB704\rtw\c\tlc\lib\sysarglib.tlc Line: 197 Column: 9 Undefined identifier TriggerTID
Workaround: Do one of the following:Set the Inline parameters on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.Delete the following lines (line 871-873) from matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/setuplib.tlc.%if SkipBlockFcn    %continue %endif

Summary: Updating a model that references a model with unlabeled output ports can cause a segV
Description: The following segmentation violation can occur when updating or simulating a Simulink model that contains a Model Reference block and the model being referenced does not label any of the root output signals. Stack Trace:   [0] simulink.dll:struct slErrMsg_tag * __cdecl ModelrefValidateSignalLabel(struct slBlock_tag *,int,enum InputOrOutput,struct slBlock_tag *,int)(0, 1, 0x1ebd7100, 0) + 131 bytes  [1] simulink.dll:struct slErrMsg_tag * __cdecl  ModelRefCheckAndSetOutputRTWStorageClass(struct slBlock_tag *)(8, 0x00cda9c8, 0x79d93015, 0x1ebd7100) + 139 bytes   [2] simulink.dll:struct slErrMsg_tag * __cdecl  ModelRefInputOutputCheckAndSetRTWStorageClass(struct slBlock_tag *)
Workaround: Label the root output ports of the referenced model.

Summary: Subsystem right-click builds: can fail if a signal using custom storage class is part of virtual bus output
Description: Subsystem right-click builds can fail if the following conditions exist: An output port of the selected subsystem block is a virtual bus  One or more signals that are part of the virtual bus have a custom storage class the resolves to a Simulink signal object

Summary: Restarted job manager requires that you recreate client objects
Description: If you stop a job manager and start it again, or if it crashes and recovers, the existing objects in your client MATLAB session can no longer access the objects in that job manager.
Workaround: To work around this issue, you must recreate the objects in the client session that reference that job manager and its jobs and tasks.  Use findResource to create the job manager object in your workspace again.

Summary: Incorrect synchronization for transitions that cross subchart boundaries
Description: Transitions that cross subchart boundaries are duplicated in the DOORS surrogate module and are indicated as deleted in subsequent synchronizations.

Summary: Comment strings: comments generated from block property descriptions missing newline characters
Description: If you define two lines in the Description  field of the General  tab in a Block Property dialog box, Real-Time Workshop omits the newline character and beginning "#" character from the corresponding comment in the generated code for that block.

Summary: Subsystems: an error can occur during code generation
Description: If you try to right-click build a Subsystem block that uses a virtual bus for an I/O port and one or more of the bus signals has a storage class other than Auto , Real-Time Workshop can fail and display the  following error messages while generating code from a subsystem:    Selected signal 'name' in 'BlockName' is not part of the bus entering   the Bus Selector   The name '' specified for the signal from output port 1 of  'BlockName' is not a valid identifier.

Summary: Simulink.DataType HeaderFile property: ignored for external mode code generation
Description: Real-Time Workshop ignores the HeaderFile property of a Simulink.DataType object for external mode code generation.  This can make it difficult to integrate legacy code.

Summary: HeaderFile property of Simulink data type objects not honored when generating S-function wrapper for ERT target
Description: If you use the Create Simulink (S-Function) block  option in the Interface  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog to automatically generate an S-function wrapper for an ERT target (for example for software-in-the-loop (SIL) code verification or simulation acceleration), Real-Time Workshop Embebbed Coder ignores the HeaderFile  property of Simulink data type objects (e.g. Simulink.AliasType  or Simulink.NumericType  objects), or Simulink.Bus  objects when generating code.

Summary: evalin fails to execute expressions requiring context of the called function
Description: The statement evalin(workspace, expression) executes the given MATLAB expression using variables taken from a workspace outside the scope of the currently executing function. When the first argument to evalin is the string 'caller', these variables are taken from the workspace of the calling function. Any changes to the values of these variables are also written back to the workspace of the calling function. When the first argument is 'base', the workspace holding variables defined at the MATLAB command line is used. The intended behavior of evalin used with either workspace type is that it evaluate only variables  from the specified workspace. It should evaluate the functional part of the expression in the context of the function in which the evalin statement appears, and not in the context of the calling function or command line. In MATLAB R14 and R14SP1, a bug in evalin causes it to evaluate the entire expression  in the context of the calling function or the command line. For example, the statement evalin('caller', 'myFun(a)') would dispatch to a subfunction myFun defined within the caller's MATLAB code file rather than the intended subfunction of the same name in the called function's file. As a result of this bug, evalin sometimes failed to execute expressions that required the context of the called function. Example 1:  This example uses evalin with 'caller' first to operate on variables in the calling function, and then to execute the mfilename function. evalin should only use the variables  (a and b) from the workspace of the caller; it should not evaluate the filename function in the caller. When this example operates correctly, evalFun_1A returns the sum of a and b, and then the filename of the called function, evalFun_1B. Here are the two functions:    ---------- FILE evalFun_1A.m ----------   function evalFun_1A   a = 100;  b = 400;   evalFun_1B   ---------------------------------------   ---------- FILE evalFun_1B.m ----------   function evalFun_1B   x = evalin('caller', 'a + b');   s = evalin('caller', 'filename');   disp(sprintf('   Value is %d.  Current file is %s', x, s))   --------------------------------------- R14SP1 evaluates a + b correctly, but unexpectedly evaluated the filename function in the caller's context, thus returning the wrong answer, evalFun_1A:    evalFun_1A      Value is 500.  Current file is evalFun_1A Example 2:  This example shows how the bug in evalin can cause a conflict when calling two functions of the same name, one a function and the other a subfunction. The evalin expression is evaluated in the base workspace. The expression includes a function call and two variables:     evalin('base', 'evalFun_2B(a, b)') Only the variables should be evaluated in the base workspace. MATLAB should evaluate the call to evalFun_2B in the workspace of function evalFun_2A and thus call the subfunction  evalFun_2B, and not the primary function  evalFun_2B:    ---------- FILE evalFun_2A.m ----------   function evalFun_2A   evalin('base', 'evalFun_2B(a, b)')    function x = evalFun_2B(in1, in2)   disp 'SUBFUNCTION evalFun_2B adds a and b'   x = in1 + in2;   ---------------------------------------   ---------- FILE evalFun_2B.m ----------   function x = evalFun_2B(in1, in2)   disp 'PRIMARY FUNCTION evalFun_2B subtracts b from a'   x = in1 - in2;   --------------------------------------- When run in R14 and R14SP1, MATLAB evaluates the call to evalFun_2B in the base workspace. Because subfunction evalFun_2B is unknown to the base workspace, the primary function is called instead:    a = 500;   b = 123;   evalFun_2A      PRIMARY FUNCTION evalFun_2B subtracts b from a      ans =         377 Example 3:  This example illustrates another way this bug can affect the rules regarding function scope. The function in Directory A (evalFun_3A) has access to private function evalFun_Priv. The function in Directory B (evalFun_3B) does not. Function evalFun_3A calls evalFun_3B (via function handle), and evalFun_3B attempts to evaluate an expression that involves the private function, even though that function is in scope only for the caller. This example "works" (even though it really shouldn't) in R14 and R14SP1 because MATLAB evaluates the entire expression 'evalFun_Priv(x, y)' in the context of evalFun_3A, where the private function is in scope. However, in R14SP2 it errors (which is the correct behavior) because the expression gets evaluated in evalFun_3B which has no access to the private function under the A directory. Here are the three functions used in this example: In Directory A:    ---------- FILE evalFun_3A.m ----------   function evalFun_3A(funHandle)   x = magic(3);   y = pascal(3);   funHandle()   --------------------------------------- In Directory A/private:    ---------- FILE evalFun_Priv.m --------   function evalFun_Priv(arg1, arg2)   arg1 + arg2   --------------------------------------- In Directory B:    ---------- FILE evalFun_3B.m ----------   function evalFun_3B   evalin('caller', 'evalFun_Priv(x, y)')   --------------------------------------- To prepare to run the example, first construct a function handle that maps to function evalFun_3B:    cd B   fhandle = @evalFun_3B;   cd ../A Now call evalFun_3A, passing the handle. In R14 and R14SP1, the function executes as would be expected if the private function were in the scope of the caller:    evalFun_3A(fhandle)     ans =        9     2     7        4     7    10        5    12     8

Summary: Large array sizes: during code generation run-time parameters can be registered incorrectly
Description: Incorrect run-time parameter registration can occur during code generation if all of the following conditions apply to a model: The model contains two or more of the following blocks: Lookup Table (n-D), Interpolation (n-D) Using Prelookup, Prelookup Index Search, or Polynomial. You select Inline parameters  on the Optimization  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. The parameter is not tunable.The parameter data type differs from the block's I/O type, causing it to be transformed to the block's I/O type. For example, if param  is [1,2,3] , the default type is double precision, and the block's I/O type is single precision. Typically, the model contains at least two blocks of the affected types whose parameters are identically dimensioned, identically typed, transformed arrays of approximately 1,000,000 or more elements.

Summary: Requirements highlighting fails with multiple Stateflow charts
Description: If a model contains more than one Stateflow chart, requirements highlighting fails.

Summary: Model using custom storage classes can fail to generate code if it contains S-functions
Description: If the following conditions exist for a model, a TLC error can occur when Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder generates code: You have defined advanced custom storage class types for the model. The model contains an S-function. The S-function's BlockInstanceSetup  function calls a TLC function, such as LibBlockParameterAddr , that refers to the custom data.

Summary: Stateflow charts: generated code can fail to compile
Description: If both of the following conditions exist, Real-Time Workshop can fail to compile the code generated for a model due to missing type definitions:  You select the Inline invariant signals  option on the Optimization  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.  The code generated for a Stateflow chart is removed for optimization because an enabled subsystem is not enabled.

Summary: Requirements report does not include Stateflow objects
Description: The bug prevents Stateflow objects with requirements from being included in the requirements report for a model.

Summary: Build process: rtwmakecfg inefficiency affects build performance
Description: A part of the Real-Time Workshop build process related to rtwmakecfg  files is inefficient and affects build performance.

Summary: bus selector dialog responds slowly
Description: The Bus Selector dialog box can respond slowly at edit time, particularly for a block in a large model that contains deeply nested buses.  For example, in such a model, the dialog box can take several minutes to open or close.
Workaround: set the configuration set's diagnostic 'Mux blocks used to create bus signals' to none.

Summary: Updating large model causes memory segmentation violation
Description: Updating a model can cause a memory segmentation violation (segV) in the function SleCompilerDataMem::~SleCompilerDataMem , especially if the model is very large.
Workaround: In releases preceding the fix, you can sometimes avoid the segV by changing model configuration or model optimization settings.

Summary: Build Subsystem fails to honor data type aliases
Description: The Real-Time Workshop right-click Build Subsystem feature does not honor data type aliases.

Summary: Simulation failures after opening SimMechanics via Simulink library browser
Description: On Windows, opening SimMechanics within Simulink Library Browser can cause the SimMechanics block library to load incorrectly. SimMechanics models executed afterward might then use blocks from the not-fully-loaded library. In that case, the simulations will fail with a Physical Modeling Assertion. For example,------------------------------------------------------------------------                        Physical Modeling Assertion------------------------------------------------------------------------Assertion Failed rp_iter != rp.end() at line 1091 of file ".\pmserialize.cpp".CS3PosStack Trace:  [0] mechanical.dll:public: __thiscall PMAssertion::PMAssertion(char const *,int,char const *,char const *)(0x245a4d50 ".\pmserialize.cpp", 1091, 0x245a5060 "rp_iter != rp.end()", 0x00cda540 "CS3Pos") + 160 bytes  [1] mechanical.dll:public: virtual class GenericRuntimeData const * __thiscall PMSerializeRuntimeParameter::generateValue(class RuntimeDataGenerator &,class pmstring const &,struct mxArray_tag const *,class CompoundStaticData const &,class pmmap > const &,unsigned long,char const *)const (0x2661c790, 0x2272f72c, 0x265ca900, 0x22dacfd0) + 353 bytes.... [etc.]
Workaround: Manually load the SimMechanics library itself before using the Simulink Library Browser.>> mechlib  % loads SimMechanics library>> simulink % opens Simulink Library Browser

Summary: Local variables: overwritten in generated code before final use
Description: Real-Time Workshop can overwrite a local variable in generated code before the variable's final use, potentially leading to a wrong answer.
Workaround: Disable the Reuse block outputs option in the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Build Subsystem fails to honor signal names and storage classes
Description: The Real-Time Workshop right-click Build Subsystem feature does not always honor signal names and storage classes.

Summary: Stateflow charts: TLC error during code generation
Description: If a model includes a reusable subsystem that includes a Stateflow chart, and the subsystem is connected to a Merge block defined with a storage class other than Auto , a TLC error occurs during code generation.

Summary: Subsystems: compilation of code for models containing many subsystem files is inefficient
Description: An efficiency issue has been identified in how Real-Time Workshop includesheader files in R14, R14SP1, and R14SP2. Although the issue does not affect correctness or cause compilation errors, it can result in a much slower compile time when many subsystems are configured to generate code into their own files.

Summary: Model reference targets are rebuilt unnecessarily
Description: When updating model reference targets, Simulink may rebuild a model reference target unnecessarily. This occurs if the target uses custom storage classes created with versions of the Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder older than Version 4.0.
Workaround: For release R14SP1/R14SP2:Download attached file attachment_253590_20051208.zip. This zip file contains processcsc.p.R14sp1 (for R14SP1) and processcsc.p.R14sp2 (for R14sp2).Unzip the file.Rename the processcsc.p.<release> to processcsc.p and replace the file in toolbox/simulink/simulink/processcsc.p in your MATLAB directory.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Displaying binary data causes crash
Description: When you import binary data and display it before running parsebinary  or a similar function, MATLAB crashes.

Summary: Invalid port/parameter mismatch warning
Description: Simulink produces an invalid port/parametermismatch error or warning for models that meet the following conditions: The value of the Port and parameter mismatch  parameter in the Model Referencing  group on the Diagnostics  pane of the Configuration Parameters  dialog box is error  orwarning . The model contains a Model block that references another model. The signal names of the root outports of the referenced model are empty.   When compiling such models, Simulink displays invalid warnings similar to the following:  Warning: Referenced model port/parameter mismatch. The Model block 'io_top/Model' is referencing model 'io_sub'. There is a port/parameter mismatch between the model block and the referenced model. The block will use the current port/parameter of the referenced model.

Summary: Large signals: code generation fails with memory allocation error
Description: Real-Time Workshop can display the following error and fail to generate code for models that include large signal dimensions (for example, 3700x3700):Error building Real-Time Workshop target for block diagram 'mymodel'. MATLAB error message: Error using ==> slbuild Error using ==> RTW.makertw.make_rtw Error using ==> tlc_c Error using ==> rtwgen Memory allocation error

Summary: Performing Indexed Deletion on Sparse Matrices
Description: In MATLAB, using the empty matrix [] on the right hand side of an assignment in conjunction with an expression like A(p,:) or A(:,q) on the left hand side deletes row p or column q from the array A. For example, thefollowing code deletes column 2 of the 3-by-3 matrix A:   A = magic(3)   A =      8     1     6      3     5     7      4     9     2   A(:,2) = []   A =      8     6      3     7      4     2 The right hand side must literally be the empty matrix [].  It cannot be a variable that has the value [], as shown here:   rhs = [];   A(:,2) = rhs   ??? Subscripted assignment dimension mismatch. In previous releases, this has not been enforced for sparse matrices. In the case of sparse matrices, any empty array on the right hand side of an assignment would perform the deletion. For example, the following expression was valid in previous releases:   S = speye(3)   S =      (1,1)        1      (2,2)        1      (3,3)        1   S(:,2) = rhs   S =      (1,1)        1      (3,2)        1

Summary: Using Scalar Complex Integers with max, min, or diff
Description: In R14 and R14SP1, using max, min or diff with complex integer inputs generated an error in most cases. However, if you called these functions with one scalar input, as shown in the following examples, MATLAB did not error.   max(int8(3+4i))   diff(int8(3+4i))

Summary: Elapsed time in triggered subsystems not defined in the block output function
Description: If the following conditions exist, elapsed time is not defined in the subsystem output function code and the system will not compile: A triggered subsystem separates it's output and update functions in order to break an algebraic loop. A block is within a triggered subsystem hierarchy which requires elasped time in the subsystem output function.
Workaround: To work around this problem, you can re-configure the block needing elasped time, for example by changing the integration method on Integrator blocks. This can cause elapsed time to be used from the update function instead of the output function. Alternatively, you can use a function-call form of a triggered subsystem which always combines the output and update functions.

Summary: C++ code generation support
Description: SimDriveline and Real-Time Workshop did not generate valid code when theLanguage  pull-down menu in the Real-Time Workshop/Targetselection  node of Configuration Parameters  was set toC++ .

Summary: Linearization of models containing nonvirtual subsystems is incorrect in some cases
Description: Simulink incorrectly linearizes a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a nonvirtual subsystem A mux or a bus signal is connected to one of the subsystem's inputs One or more channels from the input mux or bus are directly feed to the output of the subsystem

Summary: Charts containing local events generated uncompilable code with code reuse enabled
Description: Charts containing local events generated uncompilable code when code reusewas turned on. Specifically, the event broadcast functions were generated asvoid-void functions when their callee functions needed argumentsfor Dwork and other data, as in the following example:static void positionctrl_broadcast_Act_OFF_(void){  uint8_T b_previousEvent;  b_previousEvent = _sfEvent_positionctrl_;  _sfEvent_positionctrl_ = positionctrl_event_Act_OFF_Ev;  positionctrl_c1_positionctrl(rtu_PosRef, rtu_PosFbk, localB, localDW);  _sfEvent_positionctrl_ = b_previousEvent;}

Summary: Block dialog rendering within the Model Explorer
Description: A regression occurred that prevented the SimDriveline block dialogs from appearing within the Simulink Model Explorer window when you clicked on the block entry in the model tree.

Summary: Bus features may not work with buses created by Mux blocks
Description: In releases earlier than R2013b, bus objects and bus-capable blocks may not work correctly with bus signals created by Mux blocks. Starting in R2013b, you cannot create a bus signal with a Mux block.
Workaround: Use Bus Creator blocks exclusively to create buses.

Summary: Saving large variables results in corrupt MAT-file
Description: Variables requiring more than 231  bytes of storage cannot be stored in MAT-files. It is possible to create such variables on 64-bit platforms. An attempt to save such a variable results in a corrupt MAT-file.
Workaround: To work around this issue, save large variables in HDF5 format. See the documentation for hdf5write.

Summary: Incorrect simulation of frequency- and time- domain data with idsim and idsimsd
Description: Obsolete files idsim and idsimsd were not deleted as expected from the ident/ident directory and have been moved to the ident/obsolete directory.  These functions provide incorrect results when simulating with frequency- and time-domain data.
Workaround: The functions sim and simsd should be used instead of idsim and idsimsd, respectively.  These functions are now obsolete.

Summary: Stateflow requirement links duplicated when an object is copied
Description: Requirement links are being unintentionally duplicated whenever a Stateflow object is copied.

Summary: Corrected Accuracy for Double Precision in double Function
Description: The double  function does not always return a value accurate to full double precision.

Summary: MATLAB Distributed Computing Engine entities do not work on machines with multiple network interface cards (NICs)
Description: DCT and MDCE entities do not work on machines with multiple network interface cards (NICs).

Summary: Errors in geoshow with vector data on Globe displays
Description: When displaying vector data using geoshow  with the Globe display, the following error arises:??? Error using ==> plus Matrix dimensions must agree....

Summary: In Normalized mode, 2-D Correlation and 2-D Convolution blocks do not protect against division by zero
Description: If you are using the 2-D Correlation and/or 2-D Convolution blocks and you select the Output normalized convolution  checkbox, you might encounter a result containing NaNs.

Summary: Variable Ratio Gear simulation results now consistent
Description: In driveline models with Variable Ratio Gear blocks, reversing the gear directionality (flipping base and follower) and changing the gear ratio to its reciprocal could sometimes change simulation results.

Summary: Horizontal concatenation error with multiple DTED tiles
Description: Function dted  can give horizontal concatenation errors when reading multiple tiles with latitude and longitude limits thatdo not span complete tiles. The error is:??? Error using ==> horzcat All matrices on a row in the bracketed expression must have the same number of rows.

Summary: xPC Target is unable to work with Embedded MATLAB function blocks
Description: xPC Target is not able to build and download models that contain Embedded MATLAB function blocks.
Workaround: For xPC Target 2.7.1 (R14SP1+), perform the following to work around this issue. Download a revised version of two TLC files. Here are the required steps: Quit MATLABRename the following TLC files: $MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\tlc\biosig.tlc$MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\tlc\mdlxml.tlc(where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabrootat the MATLAB command prompt.) Rename the files to filename.tlc.old.Download the following attached files: biosig.tlc_xpc2_7_1mdlxml.tlc_xpc_2_7_1Rename the files to biosig.tlc and mdlxml.tlc and place the new TLC files in the same directory referenced in step 2. Restart MATLAB After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt: rehash toolboxcache For xPC Target 2.7 (R14SP1+), perform the following to work around this issue. Download a revised version of the following TLC file. Here are the required steps: Quit MATLABRename the following TLC file: $MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\tlc\biosig.tlc(where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabrootat the MATLAB command prompt.)Rename the file to biosig.tlc.old.Download the following attached file: biosig.tlc_xpc2_7Rename the file to biosig.tlc and place the new TLC file in the same directory referenced in step 2. Restart MATLAB After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt: rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Error from vmap0ui when getting boundary layer
Description: Function vmap0ui  issues the folllowing error when getting the boundary layer:Function 'cumsum' is not defined for values of class 'char'.

Summary: The pointer on the figure window is not applied correctly.
Description: Under some circumstances, the Pointer  property of the figure is not set correctly on-screen, even if get(fig_handle, 'Pointer')  returns the correct value.

Summary: xPC Target support of secondary IDE drive
Description: xPC Target was unable to communicate with the secondary hard drive of a target PC. This prevented users from accessing the file system on a secondary hard drive.

Summary: xPC Target boot disk creation in current directory names with no spaces
Description: xPC Target required that the current directory name contain no spaces for the creation of a boot disk.

Summary: mapshow
Description: Function mapshow  interchanges the X and Y coordinates when given the (X, Y, Z) syntax for a geolocated data grid, a map axes is present, and the data grid is displayed as a surface. All three conditions must be true for the problem to arise.

Summary: Stateflow incremental code generation did not detect changes in MATLAB root
Description: Large teams working on the same project oftenzip up an entire directory containing models and generatedcode, and copy it to another machine with a different MATLAB installationdirectory.In this situation, Stateflow incremental code-generation did not detectthe change in MATLAB root. As a result, the previously generated makefiles did not work and caused compilation errors.
Workaround: Delete the autogenerated sfprj directory, which causes the code to be completely regenerated for the first time.

Summary: Error when using gridm with Globe display
Description: Function gridm  causes errors when used with theglobe  display.

Summary: Release 13 user-defined Simulink data classes can convert incorrectly to Release 14
Description: If you convert Release 13 user-defined Simulink data classes that include custom storage classes (CSCs) to use the Release 14 CSC infrastructure, any data objects that were saved in a MAT-file in Release 13 will not be loaded correctly in Release 14.

Summary: Reusable function generation
Description: xPC Target was unable to generate code for models that contain reusable subsystems.

Summary: The PD2-AO-96 driver is unable to produce meaningful output
Description: The PD2-AO-96 driver does not work.

Summary: Simulating models that contain Model blocks can fail
Description: Simulating a model that references othermodels fails in the link stage if your MATLAB preferencesdirectory does not contain a mexopts.bat  file (seethe online help for the MATLAB mex  and prefdir commands for more information).
Workaround: To creates  mexopts.bat file in your preferences directory, run mex -setup and select a C compiler. If you are using a Windows system, choose the lcc compiler included with MATLAB, if your system does not include any of the other C compilers supported by MATLAB.

Summary: MinMax block: inefficent code generation
Description: For a MinMax block with a single wide input signal, Real-Time Workshop generates a line of code for each element of the signal, rather than a for  loop.

Summary: Gain in complex frequency transformations of IIR filter objects is not correct
Description: The IIR frequency transformations for complex filter objects do not preserve the gain of the filter after a transformation.

Summary: License expiration warning on blocks
Description: If your license expired, the driveline blocks of a SimDriveline model displayed a 'Bad Link' label.

Summary: The lookup service component of a job manager does not work behind a firewall
Description: When using the MATLAB Distributed Computing Engine, the lookup service component of the job manager cannot be behind a firewall.
Workaround: It is possible to run the job manager behind the firewall and its lookup service outside the firewall.To start only the lookup service on a machine outside the firewall, use the command    startjobmanager -nojobmanager -name MyLookupServiceYou can then start the job manager behind the firewall with the command    startjobmanager -nojini -name MyJobManagerYou can obtain information on the -nojobmanager and -nojini flags by typing the command    startjobmanager -help

Summary: Discrete Integrator exhibits undesirable level reset behavior
Description: In  Simulink 5.0 (Release 13) and Simulink 5.1 (Release 13SP1), the Discrete Integrator block updates its states differently when a reset occurs, depending on the type of reset. If a reset occurs, the block's output method sets the block's state and output to the block's initial condition value. Then, if the reset is a trigger reset, the block's update method updates the block's state. If the reset is a level reset, the update method does not update the state. The reset behavior for the Discrete Integrator and Accumulator blocks in the Fixed Point blockset is the same as the trigger reset behavior described above. In Simulink 6.0 (Release 14) and Simulink 6.1 (Release 14SP1), the behavior for level reset is the same as that for trigger reset. In particular the Discrete Integrator block's update method does not check for a reset condition when updating states. It update states even when a level reset occurs. This change turns out to be undesirable.

Summary: Displaying shapefiles on a map axes with geoshow
Description: Function geoshow  fails to display latitude-longitude coordinate data data from a shapefile on a map axes.

Summary: Coreco adaptor does not work with IFC 5.8
Description: The Coreco adaptor was created using the IFC 5.6 libraries and Coreco now requires version 5.8.0 of these libraries.

Summary: Reversed coefficients of allpass filters used for frequency transformations
Description: Coefficients of the allpass filters used for the IIR frequencytransformations are given in terms of positive powers of z andtherefore are reversed relative to all filters in the Toolbox.

Summary: Cannot return three outputs from the frequency transformation methods of SOS filters
Description: You cannot return more than one output argument fromthe frequency transformation methods of second-order sections filterobjects.

Summary: Error in gshhs when region contains no data
Description: Function gshhs  errors if the user-supplied region of interest contains no data and an index file is present. The error isError in ==> gshhs at 124     struc = readsome(FileID,latlim,lonlim,ifilename,subset);

Summary: gshhs clips polygons before returning them and can clip them incorrectly
Description: Function gshhs  clips the extracted polygons to the specified latitude-longitude limits, and in some cases does the clipping incorrectly.

Summary: gshhs changes geometry type
Description: Function gshhs  automatically changes the geometry type from 'Polygon'  to 'Line'  for coordinate arrays that contain more than one million points.

Summary: gshhs data with longitudes between 180 and 195 degrees
Description: Function gshhs  fails to read subsections of vector data specified as having longitudes greater than 180 and less than 195, for example the coatlines of the Aleutian Islands.

Summary: Stateflow generated code for all charts in a library, causing unwanted side effects
Description: Historically, Stateflow generated code for all charts in a libraryregardless of need, causing unwanted side effects:You could not export the same graphical function in multiple library charts. Libraries grew unnecessarily large. There was unnecessary compile overhead.

Summary: User data added in a GUIDE CreateFcn callback gets lost
Description: If you use guidata  in a CreateFcn  callback to add user data to the handles  structure, that data can not be accessed by code in the other callbacks.
Workaround: Use the guidata command in the GUI's opening function or in the object's Callback function.

Summary: Unused Embedded MATLAB Function library blocks might cause errors
Description: If you create a Simulink library model with Embedded MATLAB Function blocks that are not used in your main model, you might get error messages about the unused blocks.
Workaround: Prune your library so that all blocks are used in your main model.

Summary: ssIsSpecialSampleHit can return the wrong value
Description: The ssIsSpecialSampleHit  macro returns the wrong value when the model has both a continuous and a continuous-fixed-in-minor-step task. This can cause incorrect results for simulation based targets, such as the Simulink Accelerator and Model Reference simulation target, and for Real-Time Workshop targets, such as RSIM and S-function targets.

Summary: The chart property No Code Generation for Custom Target is obsolete
Description: The chart property No Code Generation for Custom Target  is no longer relevant and not supported.

Summary: Stateflow models with custom code failed to compile when MATLAB root directory contains spaces
Description: Stateflow models with custom code failed to compile with lcc  when MATLAB was installed in a Windows directory that contains spaces.

Summary: gshhs handling of subsection data without index file
Description: Function gshhs  fails to properly read a user-specified subsection of the vector data when an index file was not first created using the Createindex  option. This causes the output structure to be in error.

Summary: Errors in polybool due to encountering polygons with differing numbers of parts
Description: Function polybool  terminates with an error when thesecond input polygon has more rings (islands) than the first polygon.

Summary: Segmentation violation occurs in pdist for large datasets
Description: Calling the function pdist  with a data matrix input, X, that has more than 46341 rows causes a segmentation violation.

Summary: Accelerator ignores "Enable local block outputs" setting
Description: The Simulink Accelerator ignores the setting ofthe "Enable local block outputs" code generation optimization available with the Real-Time Workshop.This bug also exists in the version of the Accelerator shipped with the discontinued Simulink Performance Tools product.

Summary: Truncating using Pad and Zero Pad blocks can cause segmentation violation
Description: Using the Pad or Zero Pad blocks under certain conditions could cause a segmentation violation.  This can occur when a signal is padded along one dimension while being truncated in another dimension.
Workaround: Perform signal truncation and signal padding separately in two steps, using two separate (cascaded) blocks.

Summary: resample and upfirdn produce an error if large P and Q values used
Description: Using large values of P  and Q  in theresample  and upfirdn  functions causes these functions to error.

Summary: Job and task data are not removed from the checkpoint directory
Description: Job and task data are not being removed from the job manager checkpoint directory, even when jobs are destroyed. This causes the checkpoint directory to run out of space and gradually slows down the job manager.
Workaround: If you have run out of disk space, you may be able to obtain more free space by stopping your job manager and clearing your /tmp and /var/tmp (or C:\TEMP) directories of any temprorary files.

Summary: Check for Updates: does not find all products
Description: When you select Help -> Check for Updates , the results sometimes state that certain products were not found.

Summary: ActiveX control resize inconsistent with other HG objects in GUIDE GUIs
Description: If a GUI has ActiveX controls, those controls do not resize according to the Resize Behavior options defined in the GUI Options dialog in the same way as other HG objects, such as uicontrols. This inconsistency happens both in the Layout Editor and at run time.

Summary: Subsystems: invalid code generated when a subsystem type is changed and model is not saved and reopened.
Description: Given the following sequence of events,      Real-Time Workshop generates invalid code for a subsystem. You create or load a model initially such that a subsystem in the model is a function-call subsystem. You generate code for the model. The generated code for calling the subsystem is correct. You delete the subsystem's trigger port block. You generate code for the model again without first saving and closing the model.  At the end of this sequence, the generated code for the second build will not call the function generated for the subsystem.

Summary: Simulink crashes when inserting rate transition block
Description: Simulink can crash during simulation or code generation when trying to insert a rate transition block between rates whose greatest common divisor is a rate that occurs nowhere else in the model.
Workaround: No workaround for previous releases.

Summary: Inaccuracies in referencing vectors returned by etopo5 function
Description: Function etopo5  computes different referencing vectors for data in ASCII and binary formats. Neither referencing vector is accurate.

Summary: Model's TLC arguments specifying stack attributes not honored
Description: Code generation ignores extra TLC options  specified in the Configuration Parameters for model stack attributes (MaxStackSize , MaxStackVariableSize , and DivideStackByRate ) and instead uses the values in the target's TLC file, or applies defaults if none were specified in the TLC file.

Summary: phasedelay returns wrong answer
Description: The phasedelay  function returns incorrect data. The phase delay of symmetric FIR filters is known to be equal and constant over the entire frequency range, but the phase delay returned from this function is not constant in the stopband.

Summary: Setting hardware device type parameter causes dialog box error
Description: In previous releases, using set_param  to set the ProdHWDeviceType or TargetHWDeviceType parameters could cause an error to occur when opening the Configuration Parameters  dialog box. This could occur if the parameter was set to an unknown hardware type.
Workaround: No workaround for previous releases.

Summary: Noninlined S-functions accessing elaped time cause crashes
Description: Code generated from a model containing a noninlined S-function (i.e., an S-function that lacks an associated TLC file or when using Simulink Accelerator simulation, an S-function that does not specify the SS_OPTION_USE_TLC_WITH_ACCELERATOR option) causes a segmentation violation (segV) when the S-function accesses elapsed time. The segV can occur either in a standalone executable or while running the model in accelerated simulation mode, using the Simulink Accelerator.

Summary: Subsystem block: S-function not generated if using framed-based input signals
Description: Real-Time Workshop fails to generate an S-function from a Subsystem block that uses frame-based input signals.

Summary: Mixed Case Function Names on Windows
Description: If you compile a function called MixedCaseFcn, the generated code includes the line (some arguments omitted) mclFeval(..., "mixedcasefcn", ...); This causes a verbose, run-time warning about an inexact match for the function on Windows. In addition, the associated mixed case function for this example has only capitalized the initial letter of the function name mlxMixedcasefcn

Summary: Code generation failure with Tire block and lcc compiler
Description: Do not use the Tire block from the Vehicle Components library when generating code from your SimDriveline model using the lcc compiler.

Summary: Tire singularity at Fz = 0
Description: The model underlying the Tire block is singular when the vertical load force Fz = 0 exactly, although the physical limit Fz -> 0 exists.

Summary: The Help link for the Connection Port block does not work
Description: There is one Connection Port block common to all Physical Modeling products. It appears in each domain library.  The user's guide for each product has a block reference page for the Connection Port block, with small variations among each for each domain.  But the Help  callbacks do not work, because Connection Port has no intrinsic domain - it doesn't know which product doc to call.
Workaround: You can access the reference page for the Connection Port block by following these steps: 1. In MATLAB, click Start -> Help.2. In the left pane of the Help window, select SimPowerSystems.3. In the right pane of the Help window, under Blocks, select In Alphabetical Order.  4. Select Connection Port from the alphabetical list of blocks.

Summary: Connection Port block online help
Description: The online help for the Connection Port block did not work.

Summary: Connection Port block online help
Description: The online help for the Connection Port block did not work.

Summary: Custom targets: integer values assigned to custom option when default floating-point type selected
Description: If you use a custom target and the target has a custom option set up to use a default floating-point value, Real-Time Workshop incorrectly assigns the option an integer.

Summary: Cannot connect muxed output of a Bus Selector to a Model block
Description: Simulink incorrectly displays an error if you attempt to connect the muxed output of a Bus Selector block to the input of a Model block.

Summary: Custom target dialog: fails to work correctly when target has multiple levels of inheritance
Description: If you create a custom target that has multiple levels of inheritance, for example, the target is derived from another custom target that is derived from the ERT target, you can encounter errors while trying to apply changes in the custom target's dialog.

Summary: Negative Engine power and torque
Description: In previous versions of SimDriveline, the Diesel Engine and Gasoline Engine blocks could output negative torque and power under certain conditions because the Maximum speed  dialog field could have any value.

Summary: Error when importing data by using the "Freq. Function (Amp/Phase)" option
Description: When importing data by using the "Freq. Function (Amp/Phase)" option, the following error occurs:??? Error while evaluating uicontrol Callback.  ??? Error using ==> eq Matrix dimensions must agree.Error in ==> iduifile at 489         if ~all(size(u)==size(y))  ??? Error while evaluating uicontrol Callback.
Workaround: The data characterizing amplitude, phase, and frequency should have the sizes specified in the "Arguments" section of the BODE documentation, accessed by typing the following at the MATLAB prompt:doc bodeYou can use the RESHAPE command to resize your data appropriately.  For instance, to transform a 126 x 1 vector that characterizes "amplitude" into a 3-D array of size 1 x 1 x 126, issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:reshaped_amplitude = reshape(amplitude,1,1,126);Similarly, you can reshape "phase" such that it has the dimensions 1 x 1 x 126; "frequency" should have the dimensions 126 x 1.

Summary: DTED referencing vector when northwestern-most tile is empty
Description: When the northwestern-most DTED tile is absent for the area specified by the user's latitude and longitude limits, the dted  function returns NaN  instead of a valid referencing vector.

Summary: Display of images with rotational component in mapshow
Description: When given an image and a referencing matrix for it that contains a non-zero diagonal (i.e., has a rotational component), the mapshow  function does not correctly display the image (it is not rotated).

Summary: cftool No Longer Prompts to Save Session if Nothing Changed
Description: cftool  unconditionally prompts you to save every time you quit.

Summary: Simulink fails to recognize changes made to MATLAB code files
Description: If you simulate a model, then use an editor other than the MATLAB editor to change a MATLAB code file referenced by the model, e.g., a model callback, and then initiate another simulation of the model from the Simulink GUI, Simulink can fail to reload the edited MATLAB code file from disk, causing the old version still in memory to be used in the simulation.
Workaround: Use the MATLAB editor to edit the MATLAB code invoked in callbacks.

Summary: Deployed application that uses Image Processing Toolbox functions runs slowly
Description: When running a compiled MATLAB application that uses functions from the Image Processing Toolbox on a machine that does not have MATLAB installed, the application runs more slowly than it does before being deployed.

Summary: Block has constant sample time even though inline parameters is off
Description: If a block is in both an action subsystem and a periodic subsystem, Simulink honors the block's request to have a constant sample time even if the block should inherit some other sample time. This can cause wrong answers, for example, when changing parameters in successive runs of the accelerator mode.

Summary: Non-compilable VHDL code generated when Represent constant values by aggregates option selected
Description: VHDL compilation errors occurred when code is generated for cicinterp , firinterp , and firtdecim  filters if the Represent constant values by aggregates  option (or the equivalent UseAggregatesForConst   property) is selected.

Summary: anovan Function Output Structure Now Has Correct Coefficient Name Field
Description: The third output from the anovan  function is a structure with several fields, including a 'coeffnames'  field containing the names corresponding to the coefficient estimates in the 'coeffs'  field.  The order of the names for interaction terms is not correct.

Summary: Warning message incorrectly displayed by SLUPDATE
Description: In installations that do not include the Real-Time Workshop, running the slupdate  command on a model can cause the command to display incorrectly the following warning message: Warning: Cannot create target component.

Summary: Corrected Box Plot for Display with anova1 Function
Description: The box plots displayed by the anova1  function are shifted one position to the right, so the first tick mark does not correspond to a box and the last box does not have a tick label.

Summary: Opening a GUI with menus in GUIDE sometimes causes problem
Description: If you build a GUI in GUIDE that contains menus and then close GUIDE, you may not be able to open the GUI successfully again in GUIDE. This error is caused by the existence of menu items with no Tag  property values in the GUI.
Workaround: Open the GUI in an earlier version of MATLAB and make sure that every menu item has a non-empty Tag value. Note: MATLAB 7.0.1 (R14SP1) and later versions do not allow you to save a menu item with no Tag value set.

Summary: Issues with Video and Image Processing Blockset blocks and the Simulink Accelerator
Description: Models that contain Video and Image Processing Blockset blocks produce an error when run in Simulink Accelerator mode.
Workaround: To work around this issue, follow these steps: 1. Exit MATLAB2. Locate the following file: $MATLAB\toolbox\vipblks\vipmex\rtwmakecfg.m (where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing>>matlabrootat the MATLAB command prompt.)3. Rename the file to rtwmakecfg.m.old4. Download the following file from our Web site: https://www.mathworks.com/support/solutions/files/s1-T4I0T/rtwmakecfg.m Place the new file in $MATLAB\toolbox\vipblks\vipmex the same directory mentioned in Step 2.5. Rename the following directory$MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\vipblks\cto$MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\vipblks\c.bak6. Restart MATLAB7. Issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:>>rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Saving models containing Gauges Blockset blocks duplicates gauges
Description: When you save a model containing a block from the Gauges Blockset,a duplicate ActiveX control is superimposed over the original control. The duplication is visible if you move the control to reveal a second one underneath. When you run the simulation, the gauge appears unresponsive because the duplicate obscures the original gauge that you connected to a signal in the model.
Workaround: To prevent this problem in version 2.0:Quit MATLAB.Rename the following files by appending .old to the filename:   $MATLAB\toolbox\gauges\ax_block.m   $MATLAB\toolbox\gauges\ax_block.p$MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by entering   matlabrootin the MATLAB Command Window.Download the attached files and place them in the directory mentioned in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Enter the following in the MATLAB Command Window:   rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Using the output of a custom function as an operand for bit-shift operations caused assertions
Description: Stateflow charts with expressions containing custom C function callsand bit-shift operators caused assertions during code generation. In thefollowing example, fpga_read()  is a custom function whose returntype is unknown to the Stateflow code generator:test = int8(fpga_read(0x07) >> 5); The Stateflow coder needs to know the return type of the fpga_read()  functionto implement the shift operation properly. This situation caused assertion errors.
Workaround: Wrap the function call in an appropriate cast statement. For example, if a custom functionfpga_read() returns an int16 type, rewrite the expression: test = int8(fpga_read(0x07) >> 5); as follows:test = int8(int16(fpga_read(0x07)) >> 5);The Stateflow code generator uses the int16() cast to implement an appropriate bit-shift operation in the generated code.

Summary: Zero inertia detection
Description: In earlier versions, SimDriveline did not consistently detect and stop simulations containing zero inertia spinning about a driveline axis.  Instead, it would produce inconsistent results and errors.

Summary: freqz with [] as third input causes error
Description: Entering [] as the third input to freqz  causes an error.

Summary: Signal object consistency check can fail for signals inside triggered subsystems
Description: Simulink always reports a sample time mismatch error for a signal that resides in a triggered subsystem and references a Simulink.Signal  object that specifies a noninherited sample time for the signal.

Summary: dicomwrite cannot write PET/CT modality (SOP class) images
Description: The dicomwrite  function, when used in create mode, issues an "Unsupported SOP class" error if semantic verification is not available for the information object specified. This error occurs when attempting to write a file of a modality (SOP class) other than CT, MR, and secondary capture (SC).

Summary: Displaying datatips for scatter plots throws errors
Description: Displaying a datatip using the Data Cursor yields "Error" when clicking data point symbols in any location in a scatter plot.

Summary: Chinese IME: Exception when Typing in Command Window
Description: Typing in the Command Window with a Chinese IME causes one of the following exceptions to print to the Command Window:java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Invalid substring range at java.text.AttributedString$AttributedStringIterator.(Unknown Source) at java.text.AttributedString.getIterator(Unknown Source) at javax.swing.text.Utilities.drawComposedText(Unknown Source) at javax.swing.text.PlainView.drawElement(Unknown Source) at javax.swing.text.PlainView.drawLine(Unknown Source) at com.mathworks.mde.cmdwin.CmdWinSyntaxUI$SyntaxPlainView.drawLine(CmdWinSyntaxUI.java:509) at javax.swing.text.PlainView.paint(Unknown Source) or com.mathworks.util.Assert$AssertionException: end offset < end of prompt 275; 278at com.mathworks.util.Assert._assert(Assert.java:114)at com.mathworks.mde.cmdwin.CmdWinDocument.getPromptLineEndOffset(CmdWinDocument.java:1380)at com.mathworks.mde.cmdwin.CmdWinDocument.syntaxUpdate(CmdWinDocument.java:2706)at com.mathworks.mde.cmdwin.CmdWinDocument.postRemoveUpdate(CmdWinDocument.java:1600)at javax.swing.text.AbstractDocument.handleRemove(Unknown Source)at javax.swing.text.AbstractDocument.remove(Unknown Source)at javax.swing.text.JTextComponent.replaceInputMethodText(Unknown Source)
Workaround: If you are using MATLAB 7.0.1 (R14SP1), use the following workaround:Quit MATLAB.Download the attached patch.zip file and place the it in following location:matlabroot\javawhere matlabroot is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, which you can determine by running the matlabroot function.Extract the patch.zip file into the matlabroot\java directory. This creates a directory named matlabroot\patch into which the files will be extracted.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Japanese characters entered in Stateflow truth table UI got corrupted on model save
Description: Japanese characters entered in the Stateflow truth table UI used to get corrupted when the model was saved. Subsequent loads of the model resulted in complete loss of data in the truth table.

Summary: Bug Fix for Gamut tag clut color space transformations
Description: The applycform  function cannot apply a color transformation if the profile specified contains the Gamut  tag.

Summary: National Instruments E-series cards are not supported for 200,000 samples per second
Description: When setting the analog output sample rate on some National Instruments E-series cards under certain conditions, the toolbox erroneously gives the error "The requested sample rate exceeds the board capability."

Summary: Stateflow models containing MinMax blocks named min or max caused code generation errors
Description: Stateflow models containing Min Max blocks named min  or max  generated uncompilable code due to name collisions.

Summary: Changing slider state resets the thumb
Description: Changing the state of a slider by changing its Enable  property from off  to on  or changing the value of its SliderStep  property resets the thumb.
Workaround: After setting the Enable property on or before setting the SliderStep property, change the slider Value as shown in the code below and then change it back to its original value. h = findobj(gcf,'Style','slider');v = get(h,'Value');mx = get(h,ˇ®MaxˇŻ);mn = get(h,ˇ®MinˇŻ);set(h,'Value',(mx+mn)/2);set(h,'Value',v);

Summary: Temporary data in graphical functions shadowed global parameter names in generated code
Description: Temporary data in graphical functions intitialized from the workspace generated code that shadowed global parameter data with the same names. For example, the generated code might look like this:static real_T tmp_MyFunc(real_T In){real_T MyPrm;MyPrm = MyPrm; /* Value X */ ......

Summary: Exported graphical functions with vector/matrix input/output data caused compile errors
Description: Stateflow models containing charts that export graphical functions withvector/matrix input/ouput data generated uncompilable code during simulationand RTW code generation.

Summary: Notebook: figure windows do not automatically close
Description: When an M-book includes cells that generate figures, the figures are included in the M-book but are also each displayed in MATLAB, requiring you to close each one.

Summary: Lookup Table blocks: incorrect code generated when specified with flat 3-D and higher dimensional tables
Description: It is possible to get incorrect results when using the Flat interpolation method for 3-D and higher dimensional tables in Lookup Table (n-D) and Interpolation (n-D) Using Prelookup blocks. The bug manifests under the following set of conditions only: Flat interpolation method is used. 3-D or higher dimensional tables are used. Last dimension has more than two breakpoints. Last dimension input value is above the first breakpoint interval.  This bug exists in the twelve run-time library files named rt_lookflatn*.c .

Summary: HeaderFile property of Simulink data type object can be incompatible with the ERT 'Create Simulink (S-function) block' option
Description: If you specify the HeaderFile  property for a Simulink data type object (Simulink.AliasType , Simulink.NumericType , or Simulink.Bus ), Simulink includes the specifiedlegacy header file and uses it to obtain the typedef  for the data type, instead of generating the typedef  as it does if the HeaderFile  is not specified. Functional problems can arise if the legacy header file specifies that the data type has a memory alignment different from what Simulink expects, and if that data type is used at the interface between Simulink and generated code.  For example, if a legacy header file is used to define the type of a structure crossing the interface between an S-function generated from a subsystem and the rest of a Simulink model, and the legacy definition uses bit-packing to store that structure, the resulting memory misalignment can cause the S-function to perform improperly.

Summary: If and SwitchCase blocks: incorrect code generated when used with variable step solver
Description: If a model includes If or SwitchCase blocks and uses a variable step solver, Real-Time Workshop generates the ZeroCrossings  function incorrectly or fails to compile the generated code. This can cause a run with the Simulink Accelerator or Rapid Simulator (RSim) target to hang.

Summary: Subsystems: right-click build fails when bus signal labels do not match bus object element names
Description: Right-click build operations on subsystems fail when both of the following conditions exist: A subsystem includes an Outport block for which the signal specification Specify properties via bus object  is selected Bus signal labels do not match bus object element names
Workaround: Set the Signal label mismatch option on the Diagnostics/Connectivity pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box for the parent model to error. Then, address any errors that occur by properly setting signal labels.

Summary: Data type alias can incorrectly cause a switch block error
Description: If you connect signals having the same base data type but different aliases to the inputs of a switch block that requires all inputs to be of the same data type, Simulink can incorrectly report an error, depending on the order in which you connect the signals to the switch block.

Summary: Stateflow parameters could not be parented by multiple states
Description: Stateflow models containing multiple parameter data with the same name, but parented by multiple states, caused internal assertion errors during code generation.

Summary: Name collisions between exported data in Stateflow and Simulink were not detected
Description: When a Simulink model contained a parameter with an exported storage class and a Stateflow machine that exported data with the same name, the namecollision was not detected at code generation time.

Summary: cpcorr subfunction returns incorrect peak data
Description: The cpcorr  function contains a subfunction that calculates peaks. In certain cases, the peak data returned by this function is incorrect.

Summary: Code not generated for Custom Code block in an otherwise empty subsystem
Description: If there is a Custom Code block in an otherwise empty atomic subsystem, no code is generated for the Custom Code block. In Release 14SP1, if there is a Custom Code block in an otherwise empty function-call subsystem, no code is generated for the Custom Code block.

Summary: LifeSpan parameter specification causes model reference error
Description: If a top model references a model whose LifeSpan parameter is either an expression or a workspace variable, the simulation target for the model reference may fail to compile or may not match the top model's LifeSpan parameter.

Summary: Updating a diagram can cause a segmentation violation
Description: In previous releases, updating a diagram can cause a segmentation violation to occur in the function, tryResolveSrcTsRecForOutputPortRegion .

Summary: Scalar root level inport data incorrectly passed by value to model_initialize function
Description: For multi-instance ERT, scalar root level inport data is passed by value to the model _initialize  function.  As a result, values assigned to that data during root inport data initialization do not persist when the function completes its execution.

Summary: Unit Delay and Zero-Order Hold blocks are not properly handled in sample-time consistency check for root ports of reference models
Description: The check for sample-time consistency between the root ports of a referenced model and downstream/upstream blocks passes a Unit Delay or Zero-Order Hold block even if the block's sample time does not match the root port's and the block is not being used as a rate transition block.

Summary: Changing mask parameters on an open mask dialog causes a SegV error
Description: Simulink does not allow callbacks to change the parameters of an open mask dialog. This error cannot occur in versions of Simulink predating Version 6.0 because the earlier versions close an open mask dialog if a callback attempts to change its mask parameters.

Summary: Coverage display for library linked blocks
Description: Library linked blocks are not colored to indicate model coverage, and Stateflow linked charts display incorrect coverage information if the same chart was instantiated more than once.

Summary: Incorrect clutch locking at start of simulation
Description: At the start of a simulation, if a Clutch check box Lock clutch at start of simulation  was not selected, but the clutch dynamics indicated that it would immediately lock up anyway by itself (rather than first slipping forward or backward), the clutch would lock incorrectly.

Summary: Error when running an estimation on a model that contains Stateflow-related information
Description: When the user starts an estimation in the Estimation  tab for a model that contains Stateflow charts (or other Stateflow-related information), the following error is displayed: "Dot name reference on non-scalar structure."
Workaround: To work around this issue, download a revised version of the MATLAB code file. Here are the required steps: 1. Quit MATLAB 2. Rename the following file: $MATLAB\toolbox\slestim\slestguis\@spenodes\@EstimationLeaf\estimate.m (where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing %%%BEGIN CODE%%% matlabroot %%%END CODE%%% at the MATLAB command prompt.) Rename the file to estimate.m.old 3. Download the following file from our Web site: https://www.mathworks.com/support/solutions/files/s1-RW7XD/estimate.m Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 2. 4. Restart MATLAB 5. After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt: %%%BEGIN CODE%%% rehash toolboxcache %%%END CODE%%%

Summary: Error Invoking ˇ°Minimization of the banana functionˇ± Demo
Description: An error occurs when you invoke the ˇ°Minimization of the banana functionˇ± demo.

Summary: Transport Delay blocks: memory problem when used with Rapid Simulation Target
Description: Code generated for a model using the Rapid Sumulation (RSim) Target and a Transport Delay or Variable Transport Delay block with wide input can abort prematurely. The abort happens when the block tries to allocate more memory for its buffer.

Summary: From File block: incorrect code generated when block contains only one column of data
Description: Real-Time Workshop generates incorrect code when a model includes a From File block that contains only one column of data.

Summary: dfilt.fftfir setting numerator before filtering causes error
Description: For a dfilt.fftfir  object, setting the numerator before filtering with a long filter causes the function to error.

Summary: The syntax rgb2ind(rgb,n,'nodither') fails when n is greater than 256
Description: The rgb2ind  function, when used with this syntax:[x, map] = rgb2ind(rgb,n,'nodither') returns an incorrect first output argument, x .
Workaround: To work-around this problem, call rgb2ind twice.  Call it once to get a good colormap and then call it again to quantize directly against that map.For example:rgb = imread('peppers.png');[x,map] = rgb2ind(rgb,512,'nodither');x = rgb2ind(rgb,map,'nodither');imshow(ind2rgb(x,map))

Summary: Accelerating a simulation can crash MATLAB
Description: Using the Simulink Accelerator to simulate a model can crash MATLAB if the simulation target contains local variables that are too large to fit on the function call stack. This bug also exists in the version of the Accelerator shipped with the discontinued Simulink Performance Tools product.

Summary: Model Advisor: fixed-point checks fail when model includes Lookup Table blocks
Description: If you select the Model Advisor option Identify questionable fixed-point operations  for a model that includes one or more Lookup or Lookup2D blocks, the Model Advisor does not report any block information for the fixed-point checks.

Summary: Parent class constructor not called correctly for derived Simulink data classes
Description: If a user-defined Simulink data class is derived from another MATLAB class, the parent class constructor is not called. As a result, users are not able to define data classes that reuse the custom storage classes defined in a parent class.

Summary: Accessing parameters during chart initialization was not working
Description: Accessing parameters in charts with Execute at Initialization  turned on yielded incorrect results. For example, accessing parameters in the default transition paths of the chart or state entry function caused incorrect results. In this situation, the parameter data was not properly initialized at the time of chart initialization, which invoked a call to the chart's entry function.

Summary: Clutch velocity tolerance error improved
Description: The Controllable Friction Clutch block uses a default velocity tolerance  [] to indicate that SimDriveline should automatically adjust the tolerance during the simulation. However, if you use a fixed-step solver, this automatic adjustment is not available, and you must enter a numerical value for the velocity tolerance. If you do not, SimDriveline stops with an error.

Summary: Maximum stack size: when used with reusable subsystems generated code does not compile
Description: If you specify a maximum stack size (MaxStackSize ) for a model and the model reuses a subsystem, Real-Time Workshop can do one of the following:  Generate code that does not compile  Fail to generate code
Workaround: Disable code reuse when using the MaxStackSize variable by marking each subsystem as a nonreusable function.

Summary: freqrespest and noisepsd error for odd NFFT when spectrum range is whole
Description: In the freqrespest  and noisepsd  methods of dfilt  objects,an error ocurrs if NFFT is odd and the spectrum range is specified as whole.

Summary: Multi-Winding Transformer blocks in old models are delinked from the library
