Description: There was a bug with certain previous versions of the Multi-Winding Transformer block. The block was automatically delinked from the powerlib library. When you open an old model containing delinked Multi-Winding Transformer blocks, you might get simulation errors.

Summary: Unable to override zero crossing settings on blocks
Description: Selecting enable all  zero crossings on a model's Configuration Parameters  dialog box does not, as expected, override the zero crossing settings on the dialog boxes of the model's blocks.

Summary: dicomwrite removes TriggerTime field for files
Description: According to the DICOM specification, the TriggerTime  attribute should only be present if the ScanOptions  field indicates that a gated heart MR was conducted. The value CT  was not among the enumerated values listed for ScanOptions  but should have been.

Summary: dicomwrite parameters are case-sensitive
Description: Parameters passed to dicomwrite  are case-sensitive. For example, to specify the CreateMode  parameter, you cannot use createmode .

Summary: Simulink Accelerator requires S-function header file
Description: If a model contains an S-function generated bythe Real-Time Workshop, running the model in Accelerator mode requires the S-function's header file. This bug also exists in the version of the Accelerator shipped with the discontinued Simulink Performance Tools product.

Summary: Problems when loading a model (MDL-file) that contains an IDDATA SINK block
Description: When loading a model that contains an IDDATA SINK block, the input and output ports of the block are disconnected and the block is renamed to the value in the IDDATA Name parameter field.The following warnings appear in the MATLAB command window:Warning: test1_r14.mdl, line 644: Attempt to set DstPort with invalid Destination.Warning: test1_r14.mdl, line 664: Attempt to set DstPort with invalid Destination.
Workaround: To work around this issue, disable the link between the IDDATA SINK block and the libraries:Right-click the IDDATA SINK block.Select Link Options.Click Disable Link.

Summary: Highlighting items with requirements displays an inconsistent state
Description: The Highlight Items with Requirements  button on the Model Explorer shows an inconsistent state when you toggle between multiple models.

Summary: dicomwrite truncates precision of decimal string data
Description: dicomwrite  truncates decimal string metadata to a maximum of six digits of precision.

Summary: German keyboard entry of brackets into dialogs
Description: In previous versions, entering left or right bracket symbols ([ and ]) from a German keyboard into SimMechanics dialog fields was not possible.

Summary: Specifying many Bus Creator block inputs can fail
Description: Changing the number of inputs setting on the Bus Creator block's dialog box and then pressing the Apply  or OK  button sometimes resets the number of inputs to its original value. This can happen if the number of inputs is changed to a large value.

Summary: Code generation requirements for unintended blocks after a DOORS synchronization
Description: This bug causes all the blocks within a model to be included with requirement information in the generated code after a synchronization.

Summary: Rapid Simulation Target: cannot force storage classes to Auto
Description: You cannot force storage classes to Auto  for a Rapid Simulation (RSim) target. For example, you might want to do this to accommodate custom code interfacing.
Workaround: There is no workaround for this limitation.

Summary: hygepdf Function Now Computes Zero for Noninteger Data
Description: The hygepdf  function (the probability density function for the hypergeometric distribution) evaluates the hypergeometric probability density function even for noninteger data values where the formula does not apply.

Summary: Components may render incorrectly after changing Style and Position
Description: If you run a MATLAB script or function that  Creates a uicontrol object (such as a push button or edit text) Changes the object's Style and Position properties, in that order  the position change does not take effect.
Workaround: Resize the GUI vertically.

Summary: dicomread cannot handle DICOM files with extraneous image data
Description: dicomread  cannot read data from DICOM files that contain extraneous pixel data. dicomread  issues an error message.

Summary: Parse diagram attempted to compile the entire model
Description: Parse diagram  and Parse  options from the Stateflow Editor Tools  menu compiled the entire model instead of checking only the Stateflow chart(s) for syntax errors.

Summary: Japanese version: Macintosh keyboards require workaround to create \ (backslash)
Description: On Macintosh systems that have Japanese keyboards, the?  (yen) key does not create a \  (backslash) inthe Command Window and Editor/Debugger. This is used for the mldivide  function, for example. This is true for all applications running on the Macintosh OS X platform with a Japanese keyboard.
Workaround: The workaround depends on your Java version (JVM). In MATLAB, runversion -javato determine your Java version.For JVM 1.4.1, to create the \ character, typeOption+?.For JVM 1.4.2_05, to create the \ character, copy it from any texteditor and paste it into the MATLAB Editor/Debugger or Command Window.For JVM 1.4.2_06, to create the \ character, typeOption+?.

Summary: Simulating a model containing a Model block can cause a segmentation violation
Description: Simulating a model that references another model can cause a segmentation violation if you previously deleted a ToFile block from the referenced model.

Summary: Stateflow data created as input with inherited complexity caused errors
Description: When you create Stateflow data as an input using the Stateflow editor(Add->Data->Input from Simulink ...), its complexity was set to inherited  automatically.  Inheriting complexity caused several problems:Stateflow does not support complex data.  Therefore, inheriting complex data caused erroneous code to be generated.   Library charts did not support inherited properties. Therefore, creating a library from Stateflow charts with data of inherited complexity caused a confusing error about complex data to be reported.

Summary: Debugging Fails; Displays Error using ==> subsindex
Description: When debugging with the Editor/Debugger or with debugging functions, the following error might appear: Error using ==> subsindexFunction 'subsindex' is not defined for values of class struct. The error occurs because you have a function or variable named length  in the workspace or in the function you are running, which conflicts with the built-in MATLAB length  function.
Workaround: To avoid the problem, change the name of your length variable or function.

Summary: Attaching Communications Blockset scope to model causes segmentation violation
Description: If you use the Signal & Scope Manager feature in Simulink to attach one of the scopes in the Communications Sinks library to your model, then running the simulation causes a segmentation violation.

Summary: Multiproduct simulation segmentation violation
Description: Running SimDriveline demos before or after other Physical Modeling product demos (e.g., from SimMechanics) would sometimes lead to memory errors.

Summary: Optimization: removing instances of reusable subsystems from a model leads to incorrect results
Description: Real-Time Workshop generates code that produces incorrect results if all of the following conditions exist for a model: The inline invariant optimization is set (Inline invariant signals  option on the Optimization  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog). The model contains a reusable subsystem for which the Outputs and Update methods are not combined. An instance of the reusable subsystem is removed from code generation to optimize resulting code (for example, for an enable signal with a value of zero).  When an instance of the reusable subsystem is removed, Real-Time Workshop does generate an Outputs method for remaining instances of the subsystem, which leads to incorrect results.

Summary: Editor/Debugger: copying a large selection sometimes freezes MATLAB
Description: When you copy a large selection from the MATLAB Editor/Debugger, MATLAB appears to freeze for a minute or more.

Summary: Segmentation violation can occur when right-click building a subsystem
Description: Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder can generate a segmentation violation if you use the right-click mechanism to build a model subsystem and the following conditions exist: You set Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Data Integrity > Signal resolution control  to Use local settings .  The subsystem has an input or output signal defined with a custom storage class (CSC) and resolved to a Simulink signal object.

Summary: Vehicle load transfer calculation
Description: In the Longitudinal Vehicle Dynamics block, the vertical load forces were not computed correctly. In addition, the load transfer calculation induced an algebraic loop between the horizontal and vertical forces.

Summary: System Web browser sometimes fails to open
Description: When MATLAB tries to open the system Web browser, it sometimes fails and displays the following error in the Commmand Window: ??? CreateProcess failed.Error in ==> web at 287state = ibrowse(html_file); This can happen when you select items from the Help -> Web menu, run the web  function, or follow a link in theHelp browser documentation to a Web site.

Summary: ERT S-function created from right-click subsystem build may be unusable in original model
Description: When generating code for a subsystem with an ERT-based target, you can optionally select to generate the subsystem as an ERT S-function,by selecting Create Simulink (S-Function) block  on the Real-Time Workshop /Interface  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog. However, if any of the signals entering or leaving the subsystem has non-Auto  storage class, the resulting ERTS-function cannot be used in the original model or a signal label clash will occur.

Summary: Applying configuration parameter changes can cause an error
Description: If you move the Configuration Parameters  dialog to the bottom right portion of your screen and the dialog is small enough, Simulink might display the following error when you try to apply dialog changes. Error ocurred when evaluating preapply callback
Workaround: Move the configuration parameters dialog to the upper-left corner of your screen to avoid this issue.

Summary: Navigation in the Virtual Reality Toolbox viewer during recording hangs MATLAB
Description: Navigation in the Virtual Reality Toolbox viewer during recording caused MATLAB to hang.

Summary: imwrite fails if colormap is smaller than maximum value in image
Description: The imwrite  function fails when writing an indexed image to a JPEG file if the colormap specified is smaller than the maximum value of the image being written.

Summary: Tire model scaling and numerical stability
Description: The Tire block uses a universal formula to compute the horizontal force Fx from its inputs. It rescales the inputs taken from the dialog fields. This rescaling was implemented incorrectly. The horizontal tire force Fx was also numerically unstable if the vertical load force Fz was zero.

Summary: Product block may generate incorrect code when first input is boolean
Description: Incorrect code can be generated from a Product block if its first input is boolean.
Workaround: Do not feed a boolean value to the first input port of a Product block. Instead, convert the boolean value to another data type.

Summary: Comments: requirement information not included in some generated comments
Description: When you select Requirements in block comments  in the Real-Time Workshop/Comments  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog, Real-Time Workshop omits the following requirement information from comments in the generated code: Requirement information for a virtual subsystem that is expected in the comments of code for its parent nonvirtual subsystem  Requirement information for the root inport blocks that is expected in comments where the signal is used  In addition, right-click subsystem builds do not preserve requirement information in the original model.
Workaround: No workaround is available for previous releases.

Summary: Western offset in cells returned by the dted function
Description: When using the dted  function on level 0, 1 and 2 DTED data, the data grid returned by DTED is incorrectly positioned by the referencing vector. The third element of the referencing vector (refvec ) erroneously translates the longitude of the western edge by 1/2 a cell-width to the west. This means that DTED data cannot be properly registered with other geodata.

Summary: polybool no longer terminates MATLAB on Solaris
Description: Running polybool  in R14SP1 on Solaris caused a MATLAB crash, manifested as segmentation violations or assertions.

Summary: Linearization Points on Signals with Infinite Sample Times
Description: When you place linearization points on signals with infinite (constant) sample times, it can sometimes lead to incorrect linearization results.

Summary: Macintosh: file open and save dialog boxes do not have standard Macintosh look and feel
Description: On the Macintosh, MATLAB dialog boxes for opening and saving files do not have the standard Macintosh look and feel.

Summary: Requirements reporting for unintended blocks after a DOORS synchronization
Description: After a synchronization, all the blocks within a model are included in the requirements report.

Summary: Japanese Linux systems: need to set environment variable before starting MATLAB
Description: On Japanese Linux systems, before starting MATLAB you need to runsetenv LANG C

Summary: Current Directory browser: dragging file to newly created folder causes exception
Description: Dragging a file to a newly created folder in the Current Directory browser produces an exception, like the following, in the Command Window.  java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Row index out of range at javax.swing.JTable.boundRow(Unknown Source) at javax.swing.JTable.setRowSelectionInterval(Unknown Source) at com.mathworks.mde.filebrowser.FileView.selectRows(FileView.java:###) at com.mathworks.mde.filebrowser.FileView.selectRowsAndScrollOnEventThread(FileView.java:####)

Summary: Command Window printing sometimes fails
Description: In the Command Window, when you select File -> Print  or Print Selection , printing sometimes fails and an exception like the following appears in the Command Window.  java.lang.NullPointerException atcom.mathworks.widgets.text.print.PrintSettings.getLastPrintRequestAttributes(PrintSettings.java:###)at com.mathworks.mde.cmdwin.XCmdWndView.print(XCmdWndView.java:####)

Summary: Startup Error Message on Windows Systems with Chinese Locale Setting
Description: When you start MATLAB on a Windows system that uses the Chinese locale for its Regional setting, an error appears in the Command Window that begins as follows: sun.io.MalformedInputExceptionat sun.io.ByteToCharGB18030.convert(Unknown Source) at sun.nio.cs.StreamDecoder$ConverterSD.convertInto(Unknown Source) at sun.nio.cs.StreamDecoder$ConverterSD.implRead(Unknown Source) at sun.nio.cs.StreamDecoder.read(Unknown Source) at java.io.InputStreamReader.read(Unknown Source) at org.apache.xerces.impl.XMLEntityManager$EntityScanner.load(XMLEntityManager.java:3186) at org.apache.xerces.impl.XMLEntityManager$EntityScanner.skipString(XMLEntityManager.java:2948) at org.apache.xerces.impl.XMLDocumentScannerImpl$XMLDeclDispatcher.dispatch(XMLDocumentScannerImpl.java:563) at org.apache.xerces.impl.XMLDocumentFragmentScannerImpl.scanDocument(XMLDocumentFragmentScannerImpl.java:333) ...
Workaround: This bug should not affect MATLAB in any way, so you can continue running without any problems. If you want to clear the error message from the Command Window, run clc.This problem is due to the JVM used in some versions of MATLAB. To fix this problem, you can use the 1.4.2_05 JVM version with MATLAB. For instructions to change the JVM version MATLAB uses, see solution  1-1812J.

Summary: normxcorr2 returns complex valued correlation coefficients
Description: Due to roundoff error, some sets of input data caused normxcorr2  to return a complex-valued matrix of correlation coefficients.

Summary: Rapid Simulation Target: executable segmentation violations related to Variable Transport Delay block
Description: The Rapid Simulation (RSim) target executable for a model that includes a Variable Transport Delay block with a wide input signal produces a segmentation violation.
Workaround: Specify the same width for the block's buffer size parameter and input signal.

Summary: setm no longer errors after calls to contour3m
Description: Calling function setm  following a call to contour3m  causes the following error: ??? Error using ==> horzcat All matrices on a row in the bracketed expression must have the same number of rows. Error in ==> setm>setmaxes at 330                      set(children(i),'Vertices',[x y z],'UserData',savepts); Error in ==> setm at 52          msg = setmaxes(varargin{:});

Summary: handlem
Description: handlem  errored when querying objects created by the functions contourm  and contour3m . The error was: ??? Error using ==> handlem Unrecognized object name:  hggroup  This happened because contourm  and contour3m  were updated to return hggoup  handles, which handlem  did not recognize.

Summary: Misleading bus sample time mismatch error
Description: Simulink reports a bus sample time mismatch error message if a bus passes through a block that changes the bus's sample time, e.g., a Rate Transition or Unit Delay block, and the bus's associated bus object does not, as required, specify the bus's sample time as inherited. The error message is overly verbose and does not help identify the source of the problem.

Summary: VHDL Cosimulation block may fail to apply changes in Ports or Clocks panes
Description: When either the Ports  or Clocks  pane of the VHDL Cosimulation block is selected, the Apply  button can fail to apply parameter values that were edited.If the Apply  button is clicked, then the next time (and only the next time) the Update  button is clicked, it will ignore any changes on the edit row.
Workaround: Any action that causes the VHDL Cosimulation block dialog to refresh after the Apply selection will remove this problem.  You can trigger a dialog refresh by selecting g a different row in the signal list on the Ports or Clocks pane, or by clicking on a tab to change panes.

Summary: pixval errors with binary images
Description: The pixval  function errors when used with a binary image.

Summary: WVTool prints very small image
Description: Printing from WVTool prints a very small sized image.

Summary: WinTool analysis area context menu not displaying
Description: The context menu in WinTool, which is accessed by right-clicking in the analysis area, does not display.

Summary: Rapid Simulation Target: target does not fully support blocks with variable sample time
Description: The Rapid Simulation (RSim) Target does not fully support blocks with variable sample time. If the solver needs to adjust the next time hit of a variable sample time, the block owning that sample time is not informed about the adjustment. This can lead to incorrect simulation results.

Summary: Command line HDL code generation error for IIR DF2SOS filter structure
Description: Filter Design HDL Coder terminates with an error when generating code for the IIR DF2SOS filter structure. The error occurs in command line mode, when the first section is a second order section, and the third numerator of the section is set to zero.

Summary: rmpath &ltpartialpath> Now Reliably Removes Functions from the Path
Description: If you call addpath  with a partial path to a directory containing some function, then call that function, and finally remove that directory from the path, then the next call to the function still finds the function, even though which  and exist  do not.

Summary: Fixed-point autoscaling tool does not work in Stateflow
Description: Fixed-point autoscaling tool requires a Simulink Verfication and Validation license to work on Stateflow data.

Summary: Failures due to installing bootcode with incorrect target preferences
Description: If the processor variant or external oscillator frequency are incorrectly specified in the Target Preferences, this can result in downloading the wrong bootcode which will not function. No errors are shown.
Workaround: Check the Target Preferences are correctly set for processor variant and external oscillator frequency and reinstall the bootcode.

Summary: Cast before sum override does not work for IIR filters
Description: The Filter Design HDL Coder allows the CastBeforeSum  property of a a fixed-point filter object to be overridden. You can do this either by passing the appropriate CastBeforeSum  property-value pair to the generatehdl  function, or by selecting the Cast before sum  option on the on the Advanced  pane of the HDL Options dialog of the Filter Design HDL Coder GUI. This feature does not work correctly for IIR filters.

Summary: impixelinfo and impixelregion error on some double images
Description: The impixelinfo  and impixelregion  functions, and their counterparts impixelinfoval  and impixelregionpanel , output errors when used with a double image that has a CDataMapping  value of 'direct'  and that does not contain integers greater than zero.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, download the attached files and replace the existing version in your MATLAB installation with these updated versions.Copy impixelinfo.m to    matlabroot/toolbox/images/imuitoolsCopy getDataFromImageTypeFormatter.m to     matlabroot/toolbox/images/imuitools/@imagemodel/Copy getNumberFormatFcn.m to     matlabroot/toolbox/images/imuitools/@imagemodel/where matlabroot returns your MATLAB installation directory.

Summary: Model Reference: use with signal viewing subsystems results in an error
Description: If you use model reference and a signal viewing subsystem in a model, an error is reported for the noninlined S-function associated with the signal viewing subsystem in the referenced models.

Summary: Incompatible simulation targets for model references not removed
Description: If Simulink detects a Simulink/Real-Time Workshop project directory (slproj) created by a previous release that is incompatible with the current release, it displays a dialog box that asks whether to remove the old project. If you elect to remove the old project, Simulink removes the old project directory but does not remove the simulation targets built with the old project.

Summary: Subsystems: right-click generate S-function feature fails when Bus objects cross subsystem boundary
Description: Real-Time Workshop fails to generate an S-function when the following combination of conditions exist: You use a target other than ERT. A subsystem in your model includes an Outport block for which you select the signal specification Specify properties via bus object . You right-click the subsystem block and select Real-Time Workshop > Generate S-Function  from the context menu.

Summary: MPC555 PIL hangs MATLAB if the host serial port is unavailable
Description: It is possible to completely lock up the MATLAB process if an invalid host Serial Port is specified for MPC555 PIL communication.
Workaround: Check that you specify a valid host Serial Port.

Summary: PIL Target does not support ISO C
Description: The PIL target does not support ISO C. ISO C is supported for the Real-Time target only.

Summary: imshow overwrites nondefault axes
Description: The imshow  function, when called more than once, overwrites any nondefault axes in a figure and reverts to the default axes. By default, imshow  expects a figure to contain a single axes, sized to fill the figure window, honoring the value of the ImshowBorder  preference.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attachment which contains a fixed version of the imshow.m file. Here are the required steps: Quit MATLAB.Rename the imshow.m in your current MATLAB installation to any other name, such as imshow.m.old. This file resides in $MATLAB\toolbox\images\imuitools\, where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.Download the attached file, extract imshow.m and place it in the above directory.Restart MATLABAfter restarting MATLAB, issue the command rehash toolboxcache at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: upfirdn cannot filter data vector with matrix of filters
Description: In upfirdn  if your signal input x  is a vector and h  is a matrix of filters, an error that x  and h  must have the same number of columns occured.

Summary: RTW code generation failed for charts with no output data
Description: RTW code generation sometimes failed when amodel contained one or more Stateflow charts with no output data, and one or more local data test points or state test points. The error message displayed on the MATLAB command window looked like this:Error: File: d:\R14\matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\capi.tlc Line: 359    Column: 44                                                                 Undefined identifier constString                                           Error: File: d:\R14\matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\capi.tlc Line: 359    Column: 62                                                                 The + operator only works on numeric arguments
Workaround: Create a dummy (unused) output data object for the charts that had no output data.

Summary: Generating ASAP2 interface fails with pre-R14 ASAP2.Signal CSCs
Description: Code generation can fail if a model Uses a user-defined custom storage class (CSC) of ASAP2.Signal  created prior to Release 14, and Generates an ASAP2 interface  The error message reports undefined variables className  and packageName  at or near line 279 of asap2lib.tlc .

Summary: firgr fails to compile with MATLAB compiler
Description: When you try to compile the function firgr  with the MATLAB compiler, firgr  cannot find some required dependent functions and the compile process fails.

Summary: Matrox Meteor-II/1394 errors when ROIPosition property is set.
Description: If you set the ROIPosition  property in a video input object associated with a MatroxMeteor-II/1394 board and then try previewing it, you get the following error:  ??? matrox: DigControl ErrorValue is out of range(Error detected on 1394 system)

Summary: FDATool's Filter Internals pane is not updated for CIC Interpolator
Description: When you use the Minimum Word Length per Section  option (on the Set quantization parameters  pane) in FDATool when you create CIC interpolators, the information in the Filter Internals pane in FDATool is not updated correctly.

Summary: Macintosh: using folder selection dialog box sometimes terminates MATLAB
Description: On the Macintosh, using a folder selection dialog box sometimes terminates MATLAB. For example, if you select File -> Set Path  and then click the Add Folder  button, a folder selection dialog box appears, and using that dialog box can crash MATLAB.
Workaround: Use equivalent functions where feasible, such as addpath and rmpath.

Summary: imhist causes docked figure to become undocked
Description: Using imhist  in a docked figure causes subsequent plots to undock the figure.

Summary: Nonempty D matrix setting for a state space block can cause a segv
Description: If a model contains a state space block that has an empty B matrix and a nonempty D matrix, simulating or generating code from the model can cause a memory segmentation violation error.  For example, the following state space quadruple can expose this issue: {A,B,C,D} = {-1,ones(1,0),1,1}.

Summary: ERT S-function wrapper generation limitations
Description: The following limitations apply when the Embedded Real-Time (ERT) option Create Simulink (S-Function) block  is selected for model builds or when a right-click > Real-Time Workshop  > Generate S-Function  build is done and Use Embedded Coder  is selected in the Generate S-function  dialog: Continuous sample time is not supported. The Support continuous time  option does not apply to the generation of ERT S-function wrappers.  You cannot use multiple instances of a Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder generated S-function block within a model, because the code uses static memory allocation. Each instance potentially can overwrite global data values of the others.  Models that contain S-function blocks where the S-function is not inlined with a TLC file are not supported when generating an ERTS-function wrapper.

Summary: Overview Tool can only be resized from the lower-right corner
Description: The Overview Tool (imoverview ) can only be resized from the lower-right corner. Resizing the tool from any other corner causes the image in the tool to shrink until it disappears.
Workaround: To return the Overview Tool to its original size, close the tool andreopen it.

Summary: Dragging variables from the workspace into a drop area has no effect
Description: When dragging a variable from the Workspace into an empty document area MATLAB normally opens the variable in the Array Editor. Similarly, when dragging a variable from the Variable Palette into a Figure Window in edit mode, the variable is plotted.When selecting and dragging a second workspace variable and dropping it into a drop target in one gesture (mouse-pressing only once), the requested action is not performed.
Workaround: When dragging variables into drop targets, click and then release the variable.  Then, re-select the variable and drag it to the intended target.

Summary: sptool errors when loading spectra from command line
Description: An error occurs if you attempt to load spectra into SPTool using the sptool  command to create a spectrum structure.

Summary: Subsystems: reusability impacted by connectivity to other blocks in model
Description: If you select Reusable function  from the RTW system code  menu in the Block Parameters dialog box for a Subsystem block, the subsystem's reusability varies depending on its connectivity to other blocks in the model.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block at the input or output port of a reusable subsystem.

Summary: Inconsistent handling of DTED files with negative elevations
Description: The dted  function misinterprets certain negative values as null values, resulting in incorrect maps.

Summary: CodeWarrior remote connection fails without error
Description: Validation of the expected CodeWarrior Remote Connection, "MPC555DK Wiggler", does not occur. If the user types a slightly different name, such as "MPC555DK WIGGLER" then the wrong Remote Connection is selected. No error message is given but failures occur.

Summary: Some built-in blocks do not honor whether a mask is enabled
Description: Some built-in blocks do not honor whether a mask is enabled. These blocks are: Bus Creator Bus Selector Data Store Memory Data Store Read Data Store Write From GoTo Lookup Lookup 2D S-Function M S-Function

Summary: Deleting Stateflow object displayed in the Model Explorer caused errors
Description: Using the Stateflow Editor to delete an object  displayed in the Model Explorer search results pane generated an error in the MATLAB command window and a request to restart MATLAB.

Summary: Model reference simulation target may fail to compile
Description: If a referenced model has a triggered subsystem with a constant sample time and the referenced model also has a continuous sample time, the model reference target may fail to compile.

Summary: Problems with projfwd and projinv functions in 64-bit Linux platforms
Description: Functions projfwd  and projinv  cause a segmentation violation on a Linux 64-bit platform when using certain projections and coordinate arrays with NaN values.

Summary: Use of custom storage classes with ERT S-function disabled
Description: Use of custom storage classes (CSCs) with the ERT S-function is disabled.  CSCs are ignored if you use them with the ERT S-function.

Summary: Engine models with speed governor
Description: In previous versions, the Engine model blocks would output nonzero torque even if the output shaft speed exceeded the maximum engine speed.

Summary: Designs using the function firgr that converged in previous releases no longer converge.
Description: Several filter designs created using firgr  (formerly gremez ) in R13SP1 demonstrated convergence problems in R14.

Summary: ActiveX controls stack incorrectly in GUIDE Layout Editor
Description: The stacking order of ActiveX controls in the GUIDE Layout Editor does not always agree with their stacking order in the active GUI. This can be seen if the ActiveX controls are overlapping with other HG objects, such as uicontrols and axes.

Summary: Model reference blocks do not propagate fixed-point alias outputs
Description: If a referenced model outputs an alias for a fixed-point type, the Model block propagates the base fixed point type to the parent model, instead of the alias.

Summary: Image resolution degrades when fan rotation increment is decreased in fanbeam
Description: Reducing the value of the FanRotationIncrement  parameter increases the number of samples used by fanbeam  to create a fanbeam representation of an image. However, when this image is reconstructed using ifanbeam , the resultant image actually has poorer image resolution. This is not the expected behavior.

Summary: Cascading a single-rate filter in front of a multirate filter may produce incorrect results
Description: In cases where a single-rate filter iscascaded (connected in series) with a multirate filter, latency introduced by input, output and pipeline registers added to the single-rate filter could cause a mismatch between the results produced by the HDL code and the results produced by the filter object.

Summary: DCAM adaptor returns monochrome 16-bit formats as little endian.
Description: The DCAM adaptor returns Y16 or Mono16 formats as little endian. According to the DCAM spec v1.30, all monochrome 16-bit formats should be returned as big endian.

Summary: Roundoff Errors in fminunc Medium-Scale Method
Description: Due to an inadequate handling of roundoff errors in fminunc  with medium-scale, a NaN  value can be produced during the line search calculation.

Summary: firpm and cfirpm compile incorrectly
Description: The firpm  and cfirpm  functions do not find the required dependent functions and fail to compile correctly using the MATLAB compiler. Some functions are missing from the compiled code.

Summary: Certain numeric format conversions produce VHDL compilation errors
Description: When generating VHDL code for fixed-point filters, conversion between certain numeric formats causes VHDL compilation errors. The problem occurs when the input and output of a type conversion or typecast operation overlap by only one bit (generally the sign bit), for example converting a S35Q30 to a S10Q4.  Depending on the rounding mode, the generated VHDL could contain illegal VHDL code.

Summary: Incorrect results produced from firtdecim filters with multiple clock inputs
Description: When HDL code is generated from direct-form, transposed FIR polyphase decimation (firtdecim) filters, with the Clock inputs  option set to Multiple   and the Add input register  option deselected, the generated test bench code does not operate correctly (although the generated filter code is correct).

Summary: Engine throttle signal limited to zero-to-one range
Description: In previous versions of SimDriveline, you could input any throttle signal to the Diesel and Gasoline Engine blocks.

Summary: Double-precision filters in Verilog simulate incorrectly in ModelSim
Description: This note describes a problem that has occurred when simulating generated double-precision filters (in Verilog) with  the Mentor Graphics ModelSim simulator. The problem has been observed to occur in ModelSim versions 5.8d and earlier. The problem is caused by the way ModelSim's handling of  the Verilog 2001 always @*  construct, in cases where one of the inputs uses the Verilog function $bitstoreal()  to convert 64-bit wire ports to the Verilog real data type.
Workaround: If you still use ModelSim version 5.8d or earlier, you can work around this problem by changing lines like the following:  always @* real_filter_in <= $bitstoreal(filter_in);as follows:  always @(filter_in) real_filter_in <= $bitstoreal(filter_in);

Summary: Incorrect output from MIOS Digital Out block
Description: The MIOS digital out block is designed to accept a vector of inputs. When this input vector does not have the same width as the number of elements in the Bits parameter, the block does not error but produces unintended output.

Summary: Probability Functions Now Return Single Type Outputs for Single Type Inputs
Description: Some of the probability functions return an output that is the same type as the data input, regardless of the type of the parameter input.  For example, the expression    p = tcdf(2,single(3)) returns an output of double  type.

Summary: Processor In the Loop (PIL) Target does not support referenced models
Description: The MPC555 PIL Target does not support referenced models.

Summary: Cannot accelerate models containing S-functions with custom type dwork vectors
Description: The Accelerator cannot build a simulation or RTW target for a model that contains an S-function that has a dwork of a custom data type does not set the option SS_OPTION_USE_TLC_WITH_ACCELERATOR  In this case, compiling thecode generated by the Simulink Accelerator causes an error.> This bug also exists in the version of the Accelerator shipped with the discontinued Simulink Performance Tools product.

Summary: firpm (was remez) convergence failure
Description: firpm  designs that converged in previous versions now fail to converge.

Summary: Installation directories with spaces in the path cause build failures
Description: Builds can fail with a MATLAB installation or toolchain installation in a directory with spaces in the path.
Workaround: Reinstall using a path without spaces.

Summary: lpc prediction order greater than signal length produces incorrect results
Description: Entering a prediction order for lpc  that is larger than thesignal length produces incorrect results.

Summary: Build fails when model contains blocks from Signal Processing Blockset.
Description: The precompiled library shipped with the Signal Processing Blockset is not compatible with the Real-Time Windows Target.

Summary: Waterfall Scope can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Running a model with an open Waterfall Scope can cause MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Buses: error generated for models with an output signal defined as a bus and configured to log output
Description: Real-Time Workshop generates a fatal error during code generation when both of the following conditions apply to the model Has at least one root model output signal that was a nonvirtual bus Is configured to log root model outputs to the workspace

Summary: Rapic Simulation Target: minimum step size violations terminate RSim executables
Description: When a Rapid Simulation (RSim) target executable encounters a minimum step violation error, execution terminates abruptly.  During simulation in Simulink no such termination occurs. The linked library rsim_engine.dll  fails to handle minium step violation errors based on use of the Min step size violation  diagnostic setting the way Simulink handles the same errors during normal mode simulation.

Summary: Cannot use Image Tool zoom buttons on image with superimposed vector data
Description: Using the Image Tool, if you click on the Zoom button and then click on an area of the displayed image where vector data is superimposed (such as a sine wave), zooming does not work. If you click on another part of the image that is not covered by vector data, zooming works.

Summary: Pixel Region Tool position rectangle not draggable in scroll panel
Description: Interactions between an Image Scroll panel (imscrollpanel ) and a Pixel Region panel (impixelregionpanel ), when used in the same figure, can render the Pixel Region position rectangle unusable. The Pixel Region position rectangle, which is located in the Image scroll panel, cannot be dragged within the panel.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, use the following command after creating all of the panels needed for your GUI:uistack(hPixelRegionPanel, 'bottom')where hPixelRegionPanel is the handle returned by the impixelregionpanel function.

Summary: Code generation error with rtwgen
Description: Sometimes, directly running the Real-Time Workshop command rtwgen  on Simulink models containing SimDriveline blocks caused an error.

Summary: Clicking an inactive UIcontrol pop-up menu displays list and can execute callback
Description: If you click the arrow button of a pop-up menu whose Enable  property is inactive  or off , the drop-down list is wrongly displayed. It is then possible to change the selection and cause the callback specified by the menu's Callback  property to execute.
Workaround: Contact Technical Support for information.

Summary: Zoom, Pan, and Rotate do not work for axes inside a Uipanel
Description: The figure tools Zoom, Pan, and Rotate do not work on an axes that is a child of a uipanel.

Summary: SORT did not work in Embedded MATLAB Function Blocks.
Description: Although the documentation stated that Embedded MATLAB Function Blocks support the SORT command, the implementation did not make it onto the final CD.

Summary: impixelregion does not work correctly with images displayed in subplot
Description: If you plot several images in a figure using the subplot  function, and then call the impixelregion  function, the image displayed in the subplot changes position and becomes very small.

Summary: Cannot set properties in DFILTs after copying
Description: When you copy a fixed-point filter with CoeffAutocale  set to false  you cannot set the NumFracLength .

Summary: Flash application failure to execute after reset
Description: Some MPC555 counters fail to restart after a power on reset. This sometimes results in Flash applications failing to begin executing. The board has to be reset one or more additional times before the Flash application begins to execute.The issue has only ever been observed with development boards with a 4MHz external oscillator frequency.

Summary: Bus data type: inefficient code generated for global variable defining zero representation
Description: The generated data file model  _data.c  contains a variable that defines the zero representation of a bus data type, even if the variable is not used in the code. This occurs if the bus meets the following conditions:  Is nonvirtual  Has a structure defined by a bus object  Has a storage class of ImportedExtern  This leads to unnecessary consumption of global memory.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Pixel values of floating-point images are hard to read in Pixel Region Tool
Description: For some floating-point images, the pixel value text strings displayed in the Pixel Region Tool are hard to read because there is not enough spacing between the values.

Summary: The Uncertain Parameters Dialog Does Not Work on Macintosh
Description: When you open the Uncertain Parameters  dialog in Simulink Response Optimization on the Macintosh platform, it produces an error dialog box.
Workaround: You can add uncertainty to Simulink Response Optimization projects on the Macintosh platform by using the command line interface.

Summary: There is no way to cancel out of the value picker in the Adjust Contrast Tool
Description: Once you have activated the value picker in the Adjust Contrast Tool, there is no way to cancel the operation.
Workaround: To leave the value picker in the Adjust Contast, choose a value. Then, when you are back in the Adjust Contrast tool, press the Reset button.

Summary: Zoom buttons in Overview tool remain enabled when they have no effect
Description: The + and - magnification (zoom) buttons in the Overview tool remain enabled when they have no effect. For example, when the visible area is equal to the total area of the window, and zoom would have no effect, these buttons should be disabled.

Summary: Dragging after double- or triple-click does not select additional words and lines
Description: In the Editor/Debugger and Command Window, after double-clicking or triple-clicking and continuing to hold the mouse button down, dragging does not select additional words or lines as is standard behavior in other applications.

Summary: Torque Converter and zero Impeller velocity
Description: The Torque Converter block could accept and use zero Impeller velocity, leading to divide-by-zero errors.

Summary: xPC Target COM API missing method to add scopes of type file
Description: xPC Target did not include the method AddFileScope  as a member of xPCScopes .

Summary: Macintosh non-US keyboards: you cannot enter brackets and other extended characters in Editor/Debugger
Description: For some non-US keyboards, you cannot enter brackets [ ], curly braces { }, and some other extended characters, such as the Umlaute(? the Editor/Debugger.  This bug always occurs in Version 7.0. In Version 7.0.1 it only occurs after installing the Apple Java 1.4.2 Update 1 release (1.4.2_05 or newer JVM) on the Macintosh operating system.
Workaround: A patch is available for this bug for both 7.0 (R14) and 7.0.1 (R14SP1). To use it, follow these steps:Create the following directory:$MATLAB/java/patch/org/netbeans/editor/ where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabrootat the MATLAB command prompt.Quit MATLAB.If you are using MATLAB 7.0.1 (R14SP1), extract the following files from the attached zip file to the directory you created in step 1:BaseKit$DefaultKeyTypedAction.classBaseKit.classIf you are using MATLAB 7.0 (R14), in addition to the two files noted above, you also need to extract the following files from the zip file:MultiKeymap.class MultiKeymap$1.classMultiKeymap$KeymapSetContextAction.classRestart MATLAB.

Summary: Problems with ifir filter designs
Description: Some ifir  filter designs do not converge or might produce segmentation faults. In addition, some of the more advanced designs may not meet the desired stopband specifications.

Summary: System target file: garbled text string displayed in field on Japanese Windows 2000 systems
Description: When you select a system target file from the System Target File Browser on a Japanese Windows 2000 system, Real-Time Workshop displays a garbled text string in the System target file  field on the Real-Time Workshop  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Processor-in-the-Loop does not support Simulink Bus and Mux I/O
Description: MPC555 PIL does not support the use of Simulink Bus and MUX signals at the PIL subsystem boundary.

Summary: The set_param command does not accept multiple parameters for configuration set objects
Description: The set_param  command does not support setting multiple parameters of a configuration set in a single invocation as it does on other types of objects.
Workaround: No workaround for previous releases.

Summary: Joint Stiction Actuator failure on multiprimitive joints
Description: In previous versions, if you applied a Joint Stiction Actuator to a Joint with more than one primitive, the simulation might have failed. The error message indicated that the locking velocity threshold must be nonnegative, regardless of what locking velocity threshold you actually specified.

Summary: Dragging text: insertion point does not follow the selection
Description: When you drag text in the MATLAB Editor/Debugger or Command Window, the insertion point does not change to show exactly where the text will be inserted.

Summary: For Windows NT, GUIDE Layout Editor menus sometimes labeled incorrectly
Description: On Windows NT platforms, the GUIDE Layout Editor menu names and menu items may be mislabeled.  Menu names.  Items on the menu toolbar should appear as follows: File  Edit  View  Layout  Tools  Help  Menu items.  Names of menu items are correct but contain extraneous characters.
Workaround: Menu names. Click the corresponding menu name to get the desired menu.Menu items. The keyboard shortcuts work but the single character mnemonics do not.

Summary: Unable to delete an object while resizing it
Description: Hitting the Delete  key while resizing anobject in the Stateflow Editor threw an assertion.

Summary: Rapid Simulation Target: executables created from models with fixed step solvers and infinite stop time do not execute as expected
Description: Rapid Simulation (RSim) executables that you create from models configured with infinite stop time and that use fixed step solvers supplied with Real-Time Workshop terminate execution at time zero.
Workaround: Use the -tf command line option to explicitly specify a stop time for the RSim executable.

Summary: Variable step solver quietly takes steps smaller than the minimum step size
Description: A variable step solver may take a step smaller than theminimum step size and not issue a message. This might happen if the solver is taking the step at the request of a block with variable sample time (for example, a pulse generator).

Summary: imrotate cannot perform certain rotations of N-D arrays (when N > 3)
Description: The imrotate  function cannot rotate N-D arrays when N is greater than 3 and the angle of rotation is an integer multiple of 90 degrees. For these rotations, imrotate  collapses the N-D array to 3-D.

Summary: Logged signals may be sampled at too fast a rate
Description: Simulink logs signals nested below the top level in a model reference hierarchy at the fastest rate of the top-level referenced model, regardless of the actual sample times of the logged signals.

Summary: Linearization and differentiating with respect to the gravity signal
Description: In previous releases, if you selected the Input gravity as signal  check box in the Machine Environment dialog and attempted to linearize with respect to the incoming gravity signal, the linearization results were incorrect.  SimMechanics failed to differentiate with respect to the gravity signal. (This bug did not affect the more common case in which the result of linearizing with respect to parameters other than gravity depends on the value of gravity, whether or not Input gravity as signal  is selected.)

Summary: Distribution Fitting Tool No Longer Prompts to Save Unnecessarily
Description: The distribution fitting tool dfittool  prompts you to save your session when you quit, regardless of whether you have made changes since the last time you saved your session.

Summary: Connection Port block online help
Description: The online help for the Connection Port block did not work.

Summary: Constraint subsystem insertion and removal in Trimming mode
Description: SimMechanics did not consistently insert the constraint subsystem necessary for trimming when the analysis mode was switched to Trimming .  The subsystem was also not consistently removed when the analysis mode was switched from Trimming to another mode.

Summary: Joint Initial Condition Actuator blocks ignored while trimming
Description: The documentation has always stated that Joint Initial Condition Actuator blocks were ignored in Trimming mode.  But before Version 2.2.2, JICA blocks were treated inconsistently while trimming.

Summary: Cannot generate MATLAB code from FDATool for imported multirate (mfilt) filters
Description: Importing an mfilt  object into FDATool disables the Generate code  menu option, preventing you from generating your filter as MATLAB code.

Summary: The status of the Grid check box of the Impedance vs Frequency measurement tool
Description: In Powergui, if you open Impedance vs Frequency Measurement  tool and select the Grid  check box, then later close and reopen the tool, it forgets your preference.

Summary: Simulation errors with copied blocks
Description: Copying certain block types in SimDriveline led to simulation errors.  SimDriveline treated copies of a block as if they were the original block.

Summary: Zero current flow through the ideal transformer
Description: There is a known bug with the Linear Transformer block, the Saturable Transformer block, or any version of the Three-phase Transformer blocks, when the transformer is set as an ideal transformer (when both R and L are  equal 0 for the windings). If you simulate this system with a load, no current is shown to pass through the load. If you connect the voltage source directly to the secondary transformer windings, you receive a simulation error message.
Workaround: To work around the issue, try including a small per-unit resistance to the transformer windings. In addition, you can use a high shunt resistance attached in parallel to the load.

Summary: daqread returns message "Warning: The data file is corrupted.  All the requested samples may not have been read."
Description: When attempting to open a large file (greater than approximately 500million data points), the toolbox might incorrectly display themessage "Warning: The data file is corrupted.  All the requested samples may not have been read."
Workaround: A workaround is to specify a sample range using the Samples parameter to inform daqread to read in only part of the file.  For example,daqread('verylargedaqfile.daq','samples',[500000 599999])reads in 100,000 data points from the file.  See the Data Acquisition Toolbox documentation for daqread for more information on partially reading data acquisition (.daq) files.

Summary: Objective Gradient No Longer Estimated When Supplied by User
Description: When using fminimax ,fseminf ,fgoalattain , or the medium-scale algorithm forfmincon , and supplying the objective gradient but not the constraint gradient, both gradients are still internally estimated with finite differences.

Summary: Nonvirtual buses: code generated for blocks operating on nonvirtual buses is inefficient
Description: The code generated by Real-Time Workshop for a block operating on a nonvirtual bus (for example, a Delay block) includes a separate copy operation in the generated code for each bus element.

Summary: Supertransitions were discarded by some Stateflow commands
Description: Supertransitions were not being returned from queriesof the State object's sourcedTransitions  or outerTransitions properties.

Summary: cceps errors with column vector input and three outputs
Description: If the input to the cceps  function is a column vector and three outputs are requested, (such as [xh,nd,xh1] = cceps(data) ), an error occurs.

Summary: Illegal access of chart input data and external functions during initialization were not detected
Description: In charts with the property Execute At Initialization  enabled, the following illegal actions were not detected:Accessing chart input data Calling exported graphical functions fromanother chart during chart initialization   These actions often generated incorrect results and, sometimes, caused system crashes.

Summary: FVTool assorted issues
Description: Synchronization Issues  -- If you pass a filter object into FVTool (e.g., with h = fvtool(hd) ), the previous filter in FVTool is overwritten. Multistage Filters Information  -- Filter Information is not displayed for each stage of a multistage filter.Legend Placement via Command Line  -- You cannot use thelegend  method on the command line to control where a legend is placed in FVTool.

Summary: dfilt.latticearma and dfilt.latticear conversions
Description: Errors occur if you attempt to convert between dfilt.latticearma  and dfilt.latticear  filters.

Summary: Cannot use a specific rotation angle for fanbeam reconstruction
Description: The ifanbeam  function lets you reconstruct a fanbeam projection image.  However, when projection data does not span the full 360 degrees, there is no way to specify a different rotation span.

Summary: Affymetrix Runtime Libraries need updating
Description: If you have a previously installed version of the Bioinformatics Toolbox (version 2.0.1 and before), you need to update the Affymetrix runtime libraries.
Workaround: Run the installer, GdacFilesRuntimeInstall-v4.exe, from the directory$MATLAB/toolbox/bioinfo/microarray/lib/

Summary: Transport Delay block gives incorrect results for zero delay
Description: The Transport Delay block returns incorrect results when its Time Delay  parameter is set to be zero.

Summary: CSC structures configured to support bit-packing of Booleans do not support non-Boolean data types
Description: Data of non-Boolean types is not allowedin the Simulink predefined custom storage class (CSC) BitField .

Summary: Fmincon medium scale: function evaluation count incorrect in some cases
Description: The function evaluation count (iterative display and output.funcCount) in fmincon medium scale is not accurate.

Summary: Using symbolic expressions to specify model parameters can cause errors
Description: Using symbolic expressions to specify model parameters causes uninitialized variable errors if any of the symbols used in the expressions are defined in the model workspace but not in the MATLAB workspace.

Summary: Serial port objects cause crashes on dual-processor or hyperthreaded machines
Description: Writing data to the serial port object in MATLAB ondual-processor or hyperthreaded machines might cause MATLAB to crash immediately or after a period of time.
Workaround: There are no known workarounds.For hyperthreaded CPUs, disabling hyperthreading may alleviate the problem on some machines.  Adding a small pause after writing data using fwrite or fscanf (e.g., pause(0.05)) may alleviate the problem on some machines.

Summary: dsp_links and liblinks functions might behave erratically
Description: The dsp_links  and liblinks  functions might behave erratically when used with large models with a significant number of nested links.

Summary: Large matrices triggered stack overflow exceptions in Embedded MATLAB Function blocks
Description: Embedded MATLAB function blocks allocated all data on the stack.  This behavior sometimes triggered a stack overflow exception (0xc00000fd on Windows) when very large matrices were used.

Summary: Runtime failure for large flash applications
Description: When building an application to program into flash memory usingmatlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\c166\tasking\make\pc167csV80Flash\pc167csv80flash.mak,an incorrect memory model results in a runtime failure for some (larger) applications.

Summary: Graphical changes unnecessarily trigger target rebuild
Description: The Simulink Accelerator unnecessarily rebuilds the simulation target (i.e. generates and compiles code from the model) when the following items on the Format  menu change:                                                                           Port/Signal Displays  submenu                                            Sample Time Colors  Linearization Indicators   Port Data Types  Signal Dimensions  Storage Class  Testpoint Indicators  Viewer Indicators  Wide Nonsacalar Lines                                                       Block Displays  submenu Sorted Order  Execution Context Indicator       This bug also exists in the version of the Accelerator shipped with the discontinued Simulink Performance Tools product.

Summary: Parts of a block's name remain after it is dragged
Description: The Simulink editor occasionally leaves parts of a block's name on the diagram after you drag the block.

Summary: zerophase errors for odd number of points
Description: If the NumofPoints input to zerophase  is odd and is greater than 8192, an error occurs.

Summary: ASAP2 interface: file generation process does not support arrays
Description: Real-Time Workshop does not support arrays for ASAP2 file generation.

Summary: Incorrect initial output behaviour of MIOS MPWMSM Digital Out block
Description: The MIOS MPWMSM Digital Out block always initializes its pin high, whether the block mask has "Initial Value" set to 1 or 0.

Summary: daqread is slow with large files
Description: When you attempt to read a large file using daqread , it may take a several seconds to load and read in the file.

Summary: maxflat errors for large filter order values
Description: For large filter order inputs, maxflat  produces an error.

Summary: Help->Blocksets submenu sometimes fails to appear
Description: The Blocksets  submenu sometimes fails to appear whenselected from the model window's Help  menu.
Workaround: If this happens, click anywhere in the model window and thenselect Blocksets from the Help menu.

Summary: Constraint on how to specify output points
Description: In previous releases, the sim  command requires you to specify output points as integers times a fundamental step size, e.g., to specify [1:100]*0.01]  rather than [1:0.01:100] .

Summary: Stateflow charts violated Integer code only flag
Description: Stateflow charts containing floating point computations were not flagged when the Integer code only  flag was set on the RTW ConfigSet.

Summary: Some firlpnorm minimum-phase designs are not minimum-phase
Description: Some minimum phase filters created using firlpnorm  were not minimum phase.

Summary: Some Communications Blockset demos are incompatible with Real-Time Workshop
Description: Some Communications Blockset demos are incompatible with Real-Time Workshop. As a result, Real-Time Workshop cannot generate code for these demos:256 Channel ADSLDiscrete Multitone Signaling Note, however, that the alternative Discrete Multitone Signaling model, dmt_sim_alt , is compatible with Real-Time Workshop.

Summary: Assorted FDATool bugs
Description: Analysis Area Axes Resizing when Legends Activated  -- Turning on the legend in FDATool resizes the analysis area axes.Filter Names in Filter Manager  -- The Filter Manager allows more than one filter with the same name.Filter Manager Memory Errors  -- The Filter Manager produces "Out of Memory" errors. Viewing Arbitrary Group Delay Filter Design Coefficients  -- You cannot view the coefficients of an arbitrary group delay filter design in FDATool.Generate C Header  -- Using Generate C header  in the Targets  menu requires that other toolboxes be installed. The generated C code also does now indicates whethercomplex coefficients are real or imaginary.Exporting Files  -- Exported files are not saved to the directory to which you navigate using the Browse  button.  Instead they are saved to the current working directory.Realize Model  -- You cannot autoscale sum, gain, or convert blocks generated by Realize Model. (Note that Realize Model is available only if you have Simulink installed.)Pole-Zero Editor Filter Stability Display Updated  -- In the Pole-Zero Editor of FDATool, when you move poles and change the filter stability, the Current Filter Information  panel does not update to display the new stability.Loading Previously Saved Sessions  -- You cannot load in the current version of the Signal Toolbox FDATool sessions that were saved in a previous version.Printing Magnitude and Phase  -- The magnitude and phase response does not print correctly.

Summary: Simulation no longer stopped while MATLAB is paused
Description: In previous releases of the Microsoft Windows version of Simulink, simulations suspend when MATLAB code reaches a pause statement.

Summary: peekdata does not support native data format
Description: The peekdata  function does not allow specifyingdata format.

Summary: cceps, icceps, and rceps with complex input produce incorrect results
Description: The cceps , iceps , and rceps  functionsproduce incorrect results for complex inputs because the imaginary part of a complex input is discarded.

Summary: National Instruments hardware digital triggers cannot be configured specifically for rising or falling edge
Description: When using National Instruments cards, you cannot specify the trigger edge (rising or falling) for hardware digital triggers.

Summary: specgram displays an incorrectly shifted time vector
Description: When specgram  is used without an output, the x -axis time vector of the displayed spectrogram, which is produced via the imagesc  command, is incorrectly shifted.

Summary: fgoalattain No Longer Evaluates Objective Function Twice at Initial Guess
Description: When using fgoalattain , the objective function is evaluated at the initial guess in order to perform error checking, and again by the optimization solver once the goal attainment problem is transformed to an optimization problem.

Summary: fircls and fircls1 with filter order less than 3 does not meet design specifications.
Description: The fircls  and fircls1  functions allow a filter order of less than 3, but the resulting filter does not meet the design specifications.

Summary: gen2par produces inconsistent results
Description: gen2par  returns incorrect output for certain inputs, specifically when the input generator matrix has an identity matrix at its beginning and at its end.
